posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Instead of spamming the boards everytime I see some cool news, I’ll post it here. I’ll also keep track of prospects I like, new signings to ufc/dwcs/Bellator/etc. * Edited at 05.02.2022, 4:44 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Steve Erceg - eternal mma FLW champ. Has a win over fellow competitor Shannon ross. Not the best competition but good winstreak and creative on the ground Dominique wooding - really good signing for dwcs. Not sure about skill level but very deserving of a contender series appearance. Has crazy ko power. Had a rough stretch against better competition in bellator but has rebounded nicely with 3 highlight reel ko’s. Exciting fighter but hard to judge skill level. I think he would be a nice edition ti 135. Joseph pyfer - lost to stolzfus on 2019 DWCS appearance. Has some bad losses to bad competition and hasn’t beaten the greatest names. Did ko undefeated fighter in his last bout tho. Wouldn’t expect anything crazy from pyfer tho. Danilo Suzart - not the most impressive guy but he’s better than most HWs so he needs to be on this season. Ares FC HW champ. Has good cardio and tdd. Faced a guy who wasn’t that great, but had a tricky style and he was able to grind out a win. Not sure about skill level but being tough and having good cardio can get you far at HW Errison ferriera - competed on last years dwcs. For having 100% finish rate, he didn’t look that great going to decision and struggling with bummy Chinese fighter they brought on. Success on this season depends who they give him but wouldn’t expect a whole lot. Hailey Cowan - solid prospect for a division that desperately needs fighters. Pretty big for weight class and has a background as a d1 athlete (I think did gymnastics or something at Baylor). Seems ok but does have some issues with sub defense. Being athletic in a thin weight class could get her far tho. Rayanne Amanda - odd signing to be honest. Bad record, bad losses, and bad competition. She has finished a lot of her fights by sub but not sure how she will fare against better competition. Clayton carpenter - elite prospect, very happy they signed him but I think he could be in ufc rn. He’s one of those new breed of fighters like mokaev who was basically born into the sport. Background in Muay Thai (won amatuer titles), kickboxing (also won titles), ibjjf winner, and US prankration team member with a win over aaron pico. Also trains at a sick gym in mma lab. Expect huge things from this kid, very well rounded everywhere Eduardo neves - really really like this guy as a prospect in a division as thin as HW. He’s young, fast, and powerful. All five wins by first round ko and got a sick ko against another undefeated guy in LFA. Ozzy diaz - LFA middleweight champ. Got a sick ko over solid regional fighter in Bruno assis. Finished everyone as a pro and I think everyone but one as an amatuer. Not sure about overall skill level but he’s got some really nice finishing power. Most recent win was very good too. Waldo Cortes Acosta - LFA HW champ. I’m actually very unsure of where he’s at skill wise (more than these other guys). He’s fought very good competition for only having 5 fights but as sometimes shown questionable fight iq. Despite that, he still managed to get a really good ko win over Thomas peterson. Has pro boxing experience (6-4) and has a big frame. Caio machado - really doesn’t impress me but HW desperately needs prospects so he’ll have to do. Last weighed 236 so he could honestly be a lhw and could struggle if he gets matched up with a bigger guy. Also just fought really poor competition. Paulo Renato jr - not sure what to think. Long period of inactivity between 2017-2021 but came back with 3 first round ko’s. Pretty poor competition but, like most HWs, the division needs fresh blood so he’ll have to do. Shannon Ross - a bit old to be a prospect but Ross has been a long time vet of the aussie mma scene. Has fought for ACA and EMMA. Hasn’t fought in a long time and has been really unlucky with getting fights; otherwise he likely could have been in the ufc already Edgar Chairez - really exciting fighter. Has good ground game and striking. Lots of highlight reel finishes but has struggled in his career. He is coming off of two pretty solid finish wins tho so that’s good. Whoever he fights he will likely go to war with. Sandra lavado - not very good competition but only 2 losses to same person (one was a split decision). Being from Peru, she’s got a good bjj game. Need to see her fight betger competition tho but looks promising.Farid basharat - I’m always hesitant with sibling duos in mma because one is always better than the other but Farid has looked really good thus far. Hasn’t fought the best comp but has at least fought better than his brother has on the regional scene LOL. He’s got a well rounded game but is much more of a grappler than his brother. Claudia leite - if her fight against Sarah Kaufman didn’t get cancelled she likely would be in the ufc. Her competition isn’t the greatest but she’s been looking good in nacao cyborg recently. Emiliano linares - budo Sento FW champ. Fought good competition and has 5 round experience. Only loss by doctors stoppage too. He looks pretty promising. Erik silva - Lux fight league FW champ. He’s finished his last four opponents by first round sub and is on a 7 fight win streak. Willian Souza - lost his debut now on an 11 fight win streak with all finishes. Been running through competition at jungle fight. Jungle fight BW champ. I would like to see him go the distance to get a read on his cardio and also fight better comp. Kaleio romero - wrestler with good gnp and sub game training at team alpha male. Looks good on paper but he’s fought at gladiator challenge therefore he’s getting ko’ed first round George tokkos - Fury FC LHW Champ. I don’t think he will turn into a contender but I really like tuco. Trains out of a fantastic gym in Sanford and also coaches a lot of guys there so he’s getting a lot of experience. Training at Sanford helps him on his overall game as he’s primarily a grappler but has good kickboxing practice and cardio training. Karolina wojcik - loads of experience; 8-2 as a pro and 7-1 as an amatuer. Her only losses as a pro have been split decisions to cheyanne vilismas and formerly undefeated ewelina wozniak. She had a good three fight run in ksw.Connor matthews - love that my MA boys are getting some love but, with NE prospects, he’s just can crushed so hard to judge if he’s actually a quality fighter. He’s absolutely demolished his competition thus far but again, they’re cans. Has also been ko’ed twice as an amatuer. I don’t expect much but obviously I am going to be rooting for him. Matej penaz - I expect very big things out of Penaz. Former glory kickboxer. 22-3, ranked #5 MW in glory, and trains with jiri. He’s a pretty big dude and has a great striking game. His ground game has looked okay but that’s always a concern with kickboxers. I think he’s an elite striker tho. I expect him to make waves in the MW division Josefinne lundgren Knutsson- I think she may be the best woman signed this season. She looks unbelievably good especially having little mma experience. Despite only being 3-0, her opponents have been a combined 11-6-2 and none have had losing records. She had a Muay Thai/KB record of 27-5 and won gold for Sweden. She’s got elite standup but is comfortable everywhere. Oh, and she trains with Khamzat. Andres Luna Martinelli - I really like this signing. Him and his brother are both very intriguing. They’ve can crushed for the most part but andres has some decent wins. He’s a very creative grappler and is coming off of a highlight reel calf slicer. Viktoria dudakova - undefeated through pro and amateur (11-0 total). Combat sambo background and immaf experience. She’s also finished everyone as a pro. Quite promising prospect. Michael parkin - not sure what to expect as his competition throughout pro and am has been terrible. He’s ko’ed everyone he’s fought and is on a 11-0-1 streak. I don’t like that the best person he’s fought is the one person he’s drawn with. Oumar sy - really impressed with this guy. He’s basically French jon Jones but bigger. 6”5 83.5 inch reach is hella impressive. He is super exciting to watch. He’s absolutely ran through his last few opponents and has fought great comp for being 6-0. I think he could be a contender. Tereza bleda - another beast of a prospect. Undefeated through pro and am (14 fights) and has some really good wins like Magdalena sormova, mabelly lima, and lucid pudilova. She’s got a judo background and is a beast on the ground. Anvar boynazarov - I really like this signing by the ufc. I think he should have had a little more time in LFA or another good promotion but I get why they signed him. He’s on the older side and green for mma, but he is a former kickboxer and is a ******* beast on the feet. 95-27 kickboxing record with wins over giga chikadze, Fabio Pinca, and abdellah ezbiri. I think he may be too green for mma and too old to really be effective but I love that they signed him and I really hope he wins; kickboxers turned mma fighters are awesome. He could end up like alex pereira or he could end up like gokhan saki. Hoping we see the Giga rematch in the ufc tho; would be so epic. If he wins, expect the ufc to push that rematch Rodrigo Lidio - was supposed to fight at upcoming LFA event but they signed him. Finishing machine (all 12 wins by finish) and has been flatlining people on the regional scene. Mateusz rebecki - I think this is easily the best signing of the season thus far. Rebecki is one hell of a prospect and he really should be in the ufc straight up. He can wrestle, grapple, and strike. He looks very good everywhere and has fought insanely good regional competition. I don’t believe he’s fought anyone with a losing record.Jack Cartwright - one of the best English prospects not currently signed. Jack has been sidelined since 2020 and had an unfortunate last fight where he won because his opponent kept heabdutting him. ****** to see, but he’s looked very good otherwise fighting at CW and he is their BW champ. He’s a very good grappler and is also a finisher. I think he could be very good in the ufc. * Edited at 05.02.2022, 3:37 PM ET *

posts.body: Ufc fight pass put out a tweet saying bo nikal will be making his pro mma debut on fight pass on June 3rd. Not opponent or promotion listed but I would assume something like lfa. It also means that once they build him up he will likely go to the ufc so that’s nice. For those who do not know. Bo is a stud of a wrestler. He’s one of the best wrestlers ever at penn state and he’s a multiple time all American, ncaa champ, 2019 u23 wrestling champ, and is open national champ. He’s also a blue belt and trains at ATT. 120-3 in college and post college he was 23-10 with multiple wins over guys like pat Downey. He’s also 2-0 in amatuer mma making his pro debut. Top prospect to look out for * Edited at 05.03.2022, 1:17 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: John adajar - 6-1 5 kos. hast fought since 2019 but clearly the ufc has had their eye on him.Angga Hans - 13-2 4 ko 8 sub. very good grappler. was a finalist in one pride's WW tournament and then went on to become the One Pride MMA LW champ with 3 title defenses. Good experience and good level of competition Kai Lu - 7-3 4 ko 3 sub. oddly enough, hes Chinese but trains and fights out of Prague? not something you see everyday. got a nice ko in his last fight but overall hard to tell his skill level. Has some bad losses and fought shady competition too Han Seul Kim - 12-4 7 ko. a product of Korean zombie mma. hes the former IC and current double G WW champ. good ko power Won Bin Ki - 15-7 9 ko 3 sub. has quite the ugly record and has been finished a lot, but most of that stems from his early career when he was fighting better guys like bruno miranda and mansour barnaoui. 7-2 since miranda loss and double G 155 champ. Min Woo Kim - 10-2 3 ko 2 sub. ROAD FC BW champ. avenged his first loss (which, because it was a majority dec, im assuming was a bad judging call) Jung Young Lee - 7-1 3 ko 2 sub. ROAD FC FW champ. has some serious ko power despite only having 3 Kos. pretty good level of competition tooBin Xie - 8-3 1 ko 4 sub. competed on dwcs last season. I thought he won the fight but he didnt do enough. has a lot of experience in one championship. Balajin - 17-2 (sherdog says 22-3 with 6 ko 12 sub). was very happy to see balajin was competing as I think hes one of the better unsigned Chinese fighters. as with pretty much every Chinese fighter tho, his record is quite padded. I like what ive seen from him thus far tho. Mingyang Zhang - 15-6 9 ko 6 sub. He was the WLF HW tournament winner but is a LHW normally. good win streak and finishing ability. Hes very active too as hes fought 8 times since his last lost in 2019 Qiu Lun - 14-8 4 ko 4 sub. fought on last season of dwcs. wasn't very impressed and not really sure why get got dwcs look anyways with such an ugly record. Zha Yi - 18-3 6 ko 9 sub. actually fought decent competition for Chinese regionals. 7-1 in last 8 and only loss in that stretch is to keremuali (who is also competing in road to ufc) Long Xiao - 24-7 4 ko 10 sub. another dwcs fighter from last season. ill cut him more slack than the other dwcs guys because he fought a pretty solid looking prospect and was competitive with him. WLF BW champ and loads of experience despite being only 24. Keremuaili Maimaitituoheti (I almost had a stroke trying to pronounce this) - 26-10 8 ko 9 sub. despite the ugly record, he has fought very good competition and enters on a 7 fight win streak. I know for a fact he was supposed to be on dwcs last season but couldn't compete because of visa issues so cool to see hes getting another opportunity. Hes beaten 3 competitors on road to ufc (wuijiburen, long Xiao, zha yi) and overall just fought the "best" guys on the regional scene Askerbai Jinensibieke - 33-10 7 ko 11 sub. much like keremuaili, ugly record (in terms of losses) but tons of experience. hes not fighting the best guys, but at least hes fighting people with winning records. WLF FW tournament winner Wulijiburen - 20-8 3 ko 13 sub. had a very forgettable run in the ufc but has rebounded nicely going 4-1 with 4 finishes (including a flying knee and von flue choke). his only loss in that stretch is to keremuaili. I dont expect a ton from him but at least he has ufc experience. Ye dam Seo - 6-2 1 ko 3 sub. Oddly enough, she's fighting top prospect josefinne Lindgren Knutsson at this event in a quasi-dwcs match where winner gets a contract. she's only lost to ayaka Miura and weili Zhang but she's getting thrown into the deep end here against josefinne Hyun Sung Park - 5-0 2 ko 2 sub. nice, undefeated record. Double G FLW champ also went 3-0 as an amateur Jeremia Siregar - 6-2 3 ko 3 sub. one pride mma FLW champ. despite never going to decision, he has gone into the 4th round before so we know his cardio is good. pretty good level of competition too. Rama Supandi - 7-3 7 subs. hes been inconsistent, but is a very good grappler. good level of comp too. Seung Guk Choi - 4-1 1 ko. korean zombie mma product. 6-3 as an amateur. hasn't beaten anyone with much experience or of merit but his only loss is to dwcs season 7 competitor (and potentially future ufc fighter) Steve erceg. Takeru Uchiha - 5-1 5 subs. 5-1 as a pro and 5-1 as an amateur (he fought 4 times in one night as an amateur too). hes only 19 which is pretty crazy. very poor competition but having 12 fights at 19 is pretty good. fights just like tatsuro Taira. Toshiomi Kazama - 3 ko 5 sub. very good prospect. long time pancrase fighter. he would probably be in ufc/dwcs already but he got viciously flying kneed in a huge upset in his last fight. I wouldn't take much away from it because he was winning the fight until that point but yeah. Top Noi - 7-3 5 ko. Muay Thai background and got some power. struggled as he fights better comp as shown by his rizen run and picking up a majority dec in his last fight at UAE warriors. Gugun Gusman - 3 ko 2 sub. Reigning One Pride MMA BW champ. would be 9-2 but lost a fight by DQ. Wallen Del Rosario - 10-3 3 ko 1 sub. not really sure what to think here. hasn't fought since 2019 and lost to a 4-8 guy just two fights ago. pretty bad competition as well and hasn't fought for any recognizable organization. Tatsuya Ando - 13-3-1 6 ko 4 sub. a lot older than a lot of these guys at 32 but has a ton of experience. Long time shooto BW champ. 8-1-1 in last 10 and only loss was when he moved up to FW and lost from an Injury. fought very good competition as far as Japanese regional scene goes. Patrick Sho Usami - 4-1 3 ko. boxing background. only 22 and started his career last year so hes pretty green. he is coming off of a loss but that was also to a much more experienced fighter. Jeka Asparido Saragih - 9-2 5 ko 4 sub. This dude looks really exciting. had a war with fellow competitor angga Hans that he did lose, but he rebounded nicely with a first round ko. despite never having gone the distance, he has 3rd round finishes and has been to a 4th round (Hans fight). Shohei Nose - 7-2-2 5 subs. 8-0 as an amateur. had two draws to start pro career and both losses came back-to-back against two solid guys (Ryuji kudo and Hikaru yoshino). Picked up a crazy leg scissor choke in his last fight and also has pro kickboxing experience while also having a great grappling background. Jun Young Hong - 11-6-1 5 ko. a bit of a weird one but probably signed because he is the double G FW champ. Another Korean Zombie mma product. he struggled to get a good win streak going but he has a lot of championship experience and a lot of his losses are to solid guys. Roshan Mainam - 6-3 1 ko 5 sub. all three losses to better fighters (one guy beat him twice). has experience in ONE but fought poor competition. also, fought on Indian regional scene which is notoriously garbage (up there with china or most middle European countries regional scenes) Anshul Jubli - 5-0 1 ko 1 sub. 1-0 as an amateur so brings him to 6 fight win streak overall. all pro fights in same Indian regional org so competition and skill level overall is pretty suspect. Pawan Maan Singh - 7-2-1 3 ko 3 sub. avenged his draw and one of his losses which is nice to see but, again, the Indian regional scene is just sus. hes fighting good competition for what they have to offer tho. Keisuke Sasu - 9-1-1 3 ko 2 sub. solid prospect. lost early in his career now 7 fight win streak thats seen him become the shooto FW champ, beating the very good Ryuji Kudo (even though it was a majority DEC). Aili Muratbek - 9-2 4 ko. started career off 1-2 (last loss to the solid Russian Emilbek). Bullet Fly FC LW champ, fought most recent fights in WLF. ****** competition because Chinese regional scene but good win streak (8). Ahmad Suhail Hassanzada - 8-1 3 ko 4 sub. He may actually be on dwcs but Afghanistan is an asian country so he could be either really. Bad competition on the regional scene but proved himself with a huge upset over a former college wrestler in LFA. Also trains at Team Alpha Male. Koyomi Matsushima - 12-5 7 ko 1 sub. very ugly record but stiff competition. Would have been king of pancrase against the tough isao Kobayashi but lost by DQ. went on to fight for ONE where he couldn't get a win streak going but lost to Martin Nguyen and Garry tonon while also beating Jae Woong Kim, Won Il Kwon, and Marat Gafaurov so clearly, Hes been tested. Yusaku Kino****a - 5-1 3 ko 2 sub. Very, very good prospect. finished everyone as a pro an amateur (5-0 5 subs as an amateur) his only loss was a win, but they made it a DQ because he held on to the fence while stomping his opponent (stomping is legal in Rizin but apparently grabbing the cage and stomping isn't LOL). So that basically should have been a ko win. Then, he fought just last month and koed a long time vet in pancrase. Rinya Nakamura - 4-0 3 ko. By far the best signing for road to UFC. I think he stands a good chance at winning the whole thing and also making waves in the ufc. hes only 27 and has won multiple freestyle wrestling championships. Won the 2017 U23 wrestling tournament and won bronze at 2011 world championships. his competition has been very impressive too, being a combined 34-12-4. his last opponent was 23-6 so hes beating experienced guys too. I think he will be a big deal in the ufc if he wins the whole thing.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Also, not that anyone really cares but it appears Dylan Salvador has a fight coming soon. He posted a video of him warming up in Titan fc gear with the caption 🤫. Dylan is one of my favorite prospects atm. For those who do not know, he is a former kickboxer with a record of 52-17-3 and experience in WAKO, K-1, and Glory where he won their contenders tournament in 2016. He holds wins over Giga chikadze and sittichai sitsongpeon (I ******* butchered that, sorry). Funny enough, for a kickboxer he has finished 3/4 wins by submission and holds a purple belt in bjj. He’s fought relatively good competition too, fighting for Titan fc and, most recently, Eagle fc where he picked up a retirement win over the solid arman ospanov in what was a very fun and hectic fight while it lasted. Hes 4-1 as a pro but fought 3 times in one night at premier fc where he beat two guys in 2 round exhibition fights, and then fought Ali zebian in the finals where he lost by split decision. He’s someone to look out for and someone I think we could see in the ufc or another major promotion sooner than later. Plus, he’s French and we all know how the ufc wants French talent * Edited at 05.15.2022, 4:30 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Holy ****, awesome dwcs matchup slated for august 2nd, shimon shimotristky vs billy goff. Billy goff is the CES WW and MW champion (won the MW strap on short notice). He has vicious ko power (especially with his elbows) and showed incredible heart in his last fight as he was able to escape from full mount and nearly being finished to getting an awesome ko. He can wrestle too but prefers to fight standing. Was a former Bellator fighter but they released him after he ko’ed a gracie as a massive underdog (+400 I believe) You may remember shimon from last season of dwcs where we didn’t get to see much of him because he faced one mike malott who guillotined him during the first engagement. He rebounded nicely with a second round ko of former ufc fighter William macario in RCC about 6 months ago. He’s very young but looks quite promising. Cant give him too much **** for losing so quickly because if you lose to malott, you are likely getting finished in the first round. He’s better than your average Israeli prospect because he is fighting in Russia, instead of the Israel regional scene (one of the worst regional scenes there is)Unfortunately, I like both guys so I don’t want to see either lose but this should be an awesome fight * Edited at 05.16.2022, 12:29 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Its not for 3 weeks, but if anyone is interested in a great display of some awesome prospects (more so than your average LFA card lol), then I highly recommend watching LFA 133 on June 3rd. Besides the two fighters in the main event, the three fighters I personally have my eye on is: Ben Tynan, Haris Talunzic, and my personal favorite (and someone who i think just oozes potential to be a big star) benjamin bennett. Ben Tynan - theres limited tape on tynan, in fact i could only find his amateur debut, but delving into his background, im now thoroughly impressed. In his one fight, he instantly shot for a takedown, got it with ease, and just pounded on the guy for a 30 second tko. Now, why am i interested in a guy no one has heard of who i can only find one fight on? Well, my interest comes in his wrestling background. Tynan wrestled in JUCO where he was a two time nation qualifier with a record of 26-4. He then got recruited to NDSU where is accolades included being the 2017 NCAA Wrestling Championships Qualifier, 4th place in the 2017 Big 12 Wrestling Championships, and 5th place in the 2016 Big 12 Wrestling Championships. To top this all off, he went 8-0 as an amatuer fighting pretty good competition, with a combined record of 15-2. He is 1-0 as a pro and fought just three months ago with a first round ko. He is primarily a wrestler but he is learning bjj as he has 3 subs as an amatuer. Hes also won multiple amatuer championships and has finished all 9 of his opponents as a pro and amateur. Oh, and to top it all off not only is he a wrestler at HW (a very rare occurrence), he also trains at team elevation so you know his cardio is on point and hes sparring with some great HWs like Curtis Blaydes and Zac Pauga. Haris Talunzic - Haris is someone I can almost guarantee we will see compete on dwcs within the next year or so. I will admit, hes not the flashiest prospect there is but he is supremely talented. He lost his debut back in 2016 and hasnt lost since, rattling off 8 straight amatuer wins and now 4-0 as a pro. He used to train at a local gym but has since relocated to factoryx and you can really see the dividends that has paid. Haris is very well rounded everywhere. On the feet, he is more of a point fighter and he likes to pick you apart with leg kicks and jabs. He has a vast array of kicks and uses them a lot because he likes to maintain distance on the feet. He has very good striking defense and because hes always fighting art range, hes able to manuever his way out of most attacks. He doesnt always go to it right away, but he has wrestling in his back pocket. Where he is best is on the ground. Haris has great bjj and has a vast knowledge of chokes. From watching his amatuer fights to where hes at now, he has improved his bjj greatly. Im impressed with his submission defense too, as in one of his fights he escaped from a very deep guillotine and armbar. One thing that really impresses me about him is his willingness to listen to his corner. Now obviously, most fighters do this but more often than not we see fighters get carried away in the heat of a fight and flat out not listen to gameplans; well, haris does not do that. In his most recent fight, he kept looking to his corner when he got taken down and within seconds of having the information relayed to him, he was able to get out and then started attacking chokes from the front headlock. I dont want to say hes ever going to be a title challenger, but i think he is someone who could develop into a very solid fighter and I really think we will see him in the big leagues within the next year or so (depending on how things go at this event lol). I would be remiss if i didnt touch on his opponent, Bahatebole Batebolati. He is a kyrgzstani representing china. Unfortunately, I cannot find tape on him but he carries an undefeated record and has been training at sanford mma for the past year under the guidance of Li Jingliang. Should be an awesome fight Benjamin Bennett - Man, where to even start with this guy; i am so thoroughly impressed with benjamin bennett. He is 3-0 as a pro and went 17-4 as an amateur, at least thats what was reported. I watched an interview with him where he said he has 33 amateur fights! I honestly believe this because i have watched fights of his that are not listed on tapology. He even said at one point, he was doing boxing, kickboxing, and amatuer mma and fought 21 times in a year! He has fought some dogs too, beating guys like issa isakov, axel sola, and darian weeks. His only reported losses are to brendan allen, william starks, and alex martinez (the latter of the two were split decisions). He trains at a great team in team elevation Bennett just oozes confidence in everything he does; its that type of confidence that we see from guys like suga sean where he just thinks hes the man. In the cage, he is ****y as can be, taunting you and shrugging off everything thrown at him and he likes to fight up close and personal. Hes best against the cage, where he throws heavy shots at very high volume. Because of his style, hes very hittable, which can be quite concerning once he fights better competition, but from what ive seen, he just eats punches and barely reacts to them connecting. He can wrestle too. Overall, I think the huge amount of experience he has helps him in the cage. He has fought very legit competition as an amateur and now trains at a great gym with guys like magny, blaydes, and sandhagen who will all help him sharpen his game. I’m very excited to see bennett perform

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Updated list of currently signed DWCS fightersWomen's SW Viktoria Dudakova - 5-0 1 ko 4 sub. 6-0 amateur career that included winning 2019 IMMAF tournament. She's fought very solid competition (combined 18-8 record!) and looks to be very promising. Not many russian females in the ufc too so she would be a nice addition. I think she looks the best out of the signed 115 girls.Karolina Wójcik - 8-2 2 ko. 8-1 amateur with her only loss coming to Cory McKenna and then rattled off 8 wins in a row. Fought very good competition and fought for orgs like EFC, BRAVE CF, and most recently KSW where she went 2-0. her only two losses have been spilt decisions against Cheyanne Vilismas and Emilina Wozniak (formerly undefeated, yet still promising prospect). Her experience alone is very impressive and she looks good from what ive seenJacqueline Amorim - 5-0 1 ko 4 sub. Hands down my favorite female they've signed this season (besides josefinne Lindgren Knutsson but she's on road to ufc now instead of dwcs). LFA 115 champ and has dominated everyone she's fought, not leaving the first round. Very high level bjj and has won multiple IBJJF tournaments and the Abu Dhabi Open. Hard to tell exactly where her ceiling is because she hasn't fought the greatest competition + hasn't left the first round but she looks like a serious problem for the division. Maria Silva - 8-0 3 ko 3 sub. I have no idea why she was so underwhelming on dwcs, but silva really opened my eyes with her most recent performance. She fought the formerly undefeated Ewelina Wozniak at Ares FC 6 and absoluely dominated her from pillar to post. I dont know if she was nervous or wasn't aggressive because she didnt know her opponent (paprocki was a very short notice replacement) but she looked like a completely different fighter from her dwcs appearance compared to her most recent performance (well granted she did dominate on dwcs but it wasn't a very persuasive performance to say the least) If she shows up like she did at Ares, she may surprise a lot of people. Sandra Lavado - 10-2 4 sub. I must admit, I dont know a whole lot about Sandra and she's one of the few fighters this season I hadn't heard about but she looks solid. She's a grappler but hasn't fought the greatest level of competition. She's beaten some undefeated fighters but she has a lot of wins over girls who were making their debuts (including her most recent win) so that doesn't exactly inspire confidence. Her only losses are to the same, undefeated girl and her most recent loss was a split decision. Rayanne Amanda - 11-5 2 ko 7 sub. This one confused me a little bit because rayanne doesn't have the greatest record, she's fought pretty terrible competition (most recent win streak is a combined 0-7 with two girls making their debuts), and she's lost to some very sub par competition. The best thing I can say about her is she's a finisher and has high level bjj (made It to the finals of the 2017 IBJJF tournament but got easily tapped) not sure she's going to have a great time compared to the other girls the ufc signed for this season. Women's FLW (only three girls signed so far so expect at least one more, if not more girls to be signed in the coming months) Nayara Maia - 5-0-1 2 ko 2 sub. Budo Sento 125 Champ. Not the greatest level of competition (all green fighters) but hasn't fought anyone with a losing record. Also trains out of a good camp in Nova Uniao. Not really sure how she will fare. Denise Gomes - 5-1 4 kos. Insane level of competition; hasn't fought anyone with a losing record and the only fighter with losses that she's fought is in her most recent fight against Melania dudova. Dudova is admittedly not a good fighter but she has ufc experience and Gomes beat the **** out of her. She lost her debut to the decent and much more experienced fighter Gisele Moriera and hasn't lost since. She took 4 years off after that loss and has been very active, picking up all 5 of her wins in the past calendar year. Quite intrigued by Gomes, if she wins, I think she could make a splash at 125. Tereza Bleda - 5-0 2 ko 2 sub. One of the best female signings this season. Loads of experience and hasn't lost a fight ever. 14-0 through amateur, pro, and in Oktagon's exhibition tournament. Besides the tournament, bleda has finished all but two of her opponents and is Oktagon's 125 champ. Her opponents as a pro are a combined 32-13-1 and in Oktagon's tournament, she beat former ufc fighter Lucie Pudilova (who has been having a career resurgence since her forgettable ufc run), the very solid Magdalena Sormova, and another undefeated prospect in Lucia Szabova. She is comfortable anywhere the fight goes and I am incredibly impressed with her. If she wins, I think she will be a top dog at 125. Women's BW Claudia Leite - 7-2 3 ko 1 sub. Hasn't fought the greatest level of competition and, unfortunately, her toughest test got canceled as she was supposed to fight Sarah Kaufman for the PAWFC belt. Its unfortunate that got cancelled because that would have been a good indicator about her skill level and could have gotten her signed to the ufc outright. In her most recent fight, she became the Nacao Cyborg 135 champ with an impressive 5th round finish. Since there is only one other BW signed currently, the winner of that fight will likely get signed. Not sure about her ceiling but if she was lauded enough to find her way into a title fight against Sarah Kaufman, then she must be pretty decent. Hailey Cowan - 6-2 2 kos 2 sub. Solid looking prospect who probably would have gotten the dwcs look already but got set back in a massive upset loss in LFA 2 years ago. She rebounded nicely with a finish at the beginning of this year, but it wasnt against the greatest of competition. Shes very big for the division and was a gymnast at Baylor so shes very athletic and physically strong (her fight style is hulk smash after all lol) but I question her submission defense (both losses by sub) and overall the girls shes fought havent been the greatest. She could probably have some success with a win because 135 is very weak; especially if she beats leite because i think leite is the better fighter. Flyweight Clayton Carpenter - 5-0 2 ko 2 sub. Very highly regarded prospect in my eyes. Trains at an elite gym in the MMA Lab and was bred into the sport. He was a golden gloves boxing champion, won multiple muay thai titles in the us, IBJJF champ, and was on the U.S. pankration team where he beat Aaron Pico. Hes young as hell too, the skys the limit with this kid. I think when (not if, im that confident) he wins, he will be a force to be reckoned with. Phumi Nkuta - 5-0 1 ko 1 sub. Also went 5-0 as an amatuer. I think phumi is also the real deal and could also be in the ufc without dwcs. He trains at an elite gym in LAW MMA and has looked every bit the real deal in CFFC where he is their reigning champ. Hes ran through everyone and has a high level wrestling game, but can also strike if needs be. Great cardio and very diverse skillset. I also think he has a very high ceiling in the division and im also pretty confident he beats whoever the ufc matches him up with. Arman Ashimov - 12-3-1 7 ko 1 sub. Doesnt have the greatest record for a male dwcs signee but when you look into his record, his losses arent that bad. His first was in his second ever fight where he fought the much more experienced vartan asatryan, the second was a split decision loss, and the third came to former ufc signee Aleksander Doscalchuk where he lost because he broke his hand. Hes fought (and beaten) a very high level of competition as he was the former M-1 FLW champ. He picked up a nasty liver shot ko in his most recent fight too. He can wrestle, like all caucasus fighters can, but prefers to strike. Steve Erceg - 8-1 1 ko 5 sub. 2 time EMMA FLW champ. Very good grappler. Fought solid competition on the aussie regional scene and has a win over fellow dwcs signee, shannon ross. Lost his second pro fight and now on a 7 fight winstreak. Also went 7-1 as an amatuer with his only loss coming to denys bondar. I think he would be a good addition to the ufc but unfortunately, they matched him up against clayton carpenter so hes probably going to have to find another way to the UFC. Shannon Ross - 13-5 6 ko 1 sub. One of the older guys they've signed for this season but that comes from his inability to get fights. Hes had so many fight cancellations and i think the ufc has wanted to sign him for the longest time but he hasnt gotten that signature win yet so hes coming on dwcs. Former EMMA FLW champ and has a lot of experience fighting good guys. Bit of an ugly record but lots of good competition. I dont like how he hasnt fought in 2 years and is already 33, so if he does win, he will have to be pushed fast. He dodged a bullet because he was orginally matched up with top prospect charles johnson but they ended up outright signing johnson so he is without an opponent atm. Edgar Chairez - 8-2 3 ko 4 sub. Doesnt have a great winstreak but oh my god is this guy a walking highlight reel. Flying knees, spinning elbows, liver shots, you name it, hes got a finish by that method. He was going to be the UWC FLW champ in a fight that he was winning, but got gullotined in the 5th round, likely setting him back from the ufc. Hes rebounded nicely with a two fight winstreak, koing both his opponents. Not sure about his skill level but this dude is too exciting to not get a shot at the ufc. Erisson Ferriera - 11-1 10 kos. Erisson competed on the last season of dwcs and i was relatively unimpressed as he had all of his previous wins by ko and struggled to finish a pretty low quality fighter. His performance was underwhelming, and he was supposed to fight recently but the fight got canceled so they just gave him another opportunity. I get why they resigned him; 125 needs prospects, but i wasnt overly impressed with him. His success is dependent on who they match him up with. He probably gets signed if he gets a win but if its anything like his last performance, im not looking forward to it.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Bantamweight Jack cartwright - 10-0 3 ko 5 sub. Former cage Warriors BW champ. He would probably be in the ufc already (or dwcs opportunity) but his last fight had a very unfortunate ending where his opponent couldnt stop headbutting him, so he won by DQ. before that though, he very muched looked like a legit prospect and, being in cage warriors, had very tough matchmaking. He also went 4-0 as an amatuer bringing his overall winstreak to 14-0. Hes primarily a grappler but hes got good ko power too. I think hes got a good shot at having a successful ufc career. 135 is a shark tank but jack has been tested and very much looks the part of a ufc fighter. Farid Basharat - 8-0 1 ko 5 sub. Very excited to see farid getting his shot. You always have to be careful with MMA sibling duos because one sibling is always better than the other (and in most cases, one is very good and the other, pretty ******). That being said, the basharats are one of the more impressive sibling duos ive seen in a while. Javid proved me very wrong and appears to be an incredibly promising talent and his brother looks not too far behind. Funny enough, hes fought better comp on the regional scene than his brother and is also undefeated with a 12 fight winstreak stemming from his ammy career (4-0). He has a diverse skillset like his brother but does not fight like him, preferring to grapple more than strike. If hes anything like his brother, I think he is going to have an immediate impact in the ufc, but you do have to take into account that one sibling is not nearly as good as the other. Either way, im very excited to see what he can do in the ufc. Andres Luna Martinelli - 12-0 2 ko 6 sub. Reigning FFC FLW champ. Has a nice undefeated record but hasnt fought the best competition. He has some good wins but overall is fighting a mixed bag of fighters; some good, some bad, no one really notable. Very creative bjj game however. Good scrambles, good transitions, and picked up a huge win via rare calf slicer in his last outing. Granted, it was against a can but still cool to see. Very odd hes fighting another FLW at BW but good to see hes at least going to be on this season of dwcs. I want to see him face better competition but he looks like a solid prospect. Alessandro Costa - 10-2 2 ko 6 sub. Reigning LFL FLW champ. Much like his opponent on this season (luna martinelli) Nono is also a grappler. His favorite move is the armbar but he can also control you on the ground. His most recent loss was a split decision and since that loss he went on to dominate in Lux Fight League. Lux usually is hit or miss on the level of competition offered but he has been fighting people with winning records and has actually looked better and better each step up in competition he has faced. Dominique Wooding - 9-4 9 ko. Cage Warriors BW champ. I think wooding is one of the most deserving signees for dwcs as he perfectly fits the bill of what dana wants out of a signee: explosive, lots of highlight reel finishes, exciting, and has a good deal of hype on his name. He went 7-2 as an amatuer. He struggled with his first step up in competition going 0-2 in bellator but all of his losses are to solid competition (except andy young but that was early in his career). Hes rebounded nicely with 3 highlight reel kos and captured (and subsequently defended) the title in an absolute war. His last three wins have been solid but even if he wins, im not sure hes a super high level fighter. He is very exciting tho. Willian Souza - 11-1 9 ko 2 sub. Jungle fight double champ (145 and 135). Very impressed with willian. He is a career FW and if he wins he will likely go back to 145. Lost his debut to a guy with much more experience than him and hasnt lost since, going on an 11 fight win streak and finishing everybody. Pretty poor level of competition but JF usually fosters great prospects. Jose Johnson - 14-7 8 ko 2 sub. I was very very happy to see jose got a second shot at dwcs because i think he is very deserving. I dont think he is a very high level fighter but he has looked fantastic since losing to top prospect ronnie lawrence during his original dwcs run, with a massive upset win over highly touted prospect Mo miller, and following that up with a 12 second, one punch ko against dulani perry. Loads of amateur experience (24-14) and despite the ugly record, has fought very good competition. He has one punch ko power and is also massive for the division at 6”0. Im not sure he will win a contract, but im going to be rooting for him because i think hes the type of signee they would feed to high level competition like they do to thiago moises or rafael alves and hes just a fun fighter to watch. Featherweight Emilano Linares - 7-1 5 ko 1 sub. Reigning Budo Sento FW champ. Is supposed to fight this friday but i doubt thats happening as he is already signed to dwcs. Went 3-0 as an amateur too. Fought decent competition. The ecuadoreans are people to look out for, as we’ve seen the rise of guys like michael morales and chito vera. If he wins, we could see linares develop into a contender (or not, just speculating really lol). Anvar Boynazarov - 3-0 3 kos. I think this may be my favorite signing of the season. Anvar is pretty green and quite old for a prospect (33) but this is because he spent the majority of his career kickboxing, where he complied a record of 95-27-2. He holds notable wins over Fabio Pinca and a little known guy named Giga Chikadze. He hasnt fought anyone of note, but when he made his u.s. Debut, he absolutely flatlined his opponent in 21 seconds. He obviously has a very high level of striking and I get why the ufc wants him with so little experience. If he gets signed, you can bet your top dollar they will push him fast to try and get the giga rematch. I really hope he gets into the ufc but they matched him up with a grappler so not sure hes gonna be winning a contract. Also, hes training at tiger muay thai so he has a great camp around him. Erik Silva - 8-1 1 ko 4 sub. LFL FW champ. He comes in on a 7 fight winstreak and has finished his last four opponents by first round submission. Trains under the guidance of bigfoot silva. Hes got a tough test in front of him with anvar, but if he grapples he could find himself winning a contract. Wish i knew more about him but all ive see was his most recent fight where he easily won the belt. He could end up getting a very easy win, or could get brutally koed. Francis Marshall - 5-0 4 sub. Went 5-0 as an amateur as well bringing overall winstreak to 10. Trains under Kurt Pellegrino. Has been putting in the work on the long island regional scene and is a very good grappler. Striking needs some work but he has been dominant on the ground against his opponents. He was originally supposed to fight a step up in competition, but the ufc decided to just sign him instead. Kaleio Romero - 6-1 2 ko 3 sub. Went 5-0 as an amateur. He is a wrestler who trains out of Team Alpha Male. Im not sold on him winning a contract and even if he does, I dont think he will have much success in the ufc. He has mostly can crushed and has multiple fights under the gladiator challenge banner therefore I will be fading him. He also lost his biggest step up in competition, getting koed by Jonas Flok who is an above average fighter. He has fought for combate in his most recent fights but still hasnt fought anyone with a winning record, with the record of his wins being a combined 13-32. Connor Matthews - 5-0 1 ko 4 sub. Went 4-2 as an amateur also finishing everyone with 2 kos and 2 sub. I will be rooting for connor because he is from MA, but like Kaleio, I dont see him winning a contract nor having success in the ufc if he does win. He has never left the first round and has fought abysmal competition. I will say tho, he is very exciting as he comes out guns blazing (much like his teacher Joe Lauzon) and tries (and usually succeeds) in finishing you first round. Good power and I guess “good” bjj but he hasnt been tested whatsoever so im sure its not that good once he faces resistance. PLus, hes already been brutally koed twice before as an amateur. I dont expect much but at least he looks exciting. Lightweight (more will be added because lidio and rebecki are already matched up and no way two LAW fighters fight one another)Charlie Campbell - 6-1 4 kos. Went 5-0 as an amateur with 4 kos and 1 sub. Pretty impressed with charlie. Hes young and trains out of a great camp in LAW mma. Fought in bellator and most recently cffc. Fought a good level of competition as he hasnt fought anyone with a losing record. Hes a pretty big 155’er standing 6 feet tall. Has some nasty leg kicks as he has finished 2 opponents as a pro via leg kick stoppages. Nazim Sadykhov - 6-1 4 ko 1 sub. Now the third LAW MMA product we have competing on this season of dwcs. Nazim is another guy i'm thoroughly impressed by, and i think he has potential to be very good. He hasnt fought the best level of competition, but hes fought guys with solid records and experience. He went 4-0 as an amateur and after losing his pro debut, hasnt lost since rattling off 6 straight wins. Rodrigo Lidio - 12-2 10 ko 2 sub. Another guy who is old for a prospect (32) but hes currently in his prime and is one hell of an exciting fighter. Originally supposed to fight for the LFA 155 belt, he pulled out once he got offered the dwcs contract. He is currently on a 4 fight winstreak as has been rattling off highlight after highlight. Lidio has serious power as shown by his 100 finishing rate and has good cardio too as he recently got a 5th round flying knee ko to become the Future MMA 155 champ. Unfortunately, he got matched up against a beast in Mateusz Rebecki. Mateusz Rebecki - 15-1 8 ko 4 sub. Rebecki is my pick for who is going to have the biggest impact in the ufc. Rebecki has long been one of the best unsigned talents in the ufc and I honestly dont even think he needs dwcs, hes that good. He is the long time FEN 155 champ, rattling off 8 title defenses! Hes currently on a 12 fight winstreak and has ran through everyone hes fought. Hes not fighting cans either, with his opponents having a combined record of 156-52-3. If thats not top level regional competition, I dont know what is. Hes fought notable guys too, like daguir imavov, magomed magomedov (not the one you were probably thinking of but still a decent fighter), and Marian Ziolkowski. Hes gone into championship round multiple times and can fight anywhere, on the feet or on the ground. I am incredibly impressed and it wouldnt shock me if they throw rebecki to the wolves right away. I genuinely think he could debut in the top 15 right now. Welterweight Billy goff - 7-2 5 ko. Another one of my favorite signings. I dont see billy goff being a world beater if he wins a contract but, my god, is he fun to watch. He is the CES MMA 170 and 185 champ (the 185 belt he claimed on short notice) and has serious ko power. He prefers to stand and bang but he can wrestle too if needs be. His best weapon is his elbows, as hes koed most of his opponents with elbows up against the fence. He also showed a great display of heart in his last fight, escaping from full mount and nearly being finished to finishing his opponent in the second. I will say, almost getting finished by justin sumter is not a good look, but goff is as tough and as game as they come Shimon Simotristky - 8-1 4 ko 1 sub. Shimon competed on last season of dwcs but unfortunately ran into mike malott and got guillotined in under a minute. Shimon is a lot better than his dwcs appearance and has a lot of experience despite only being 21. Usually, I do not rate israeli prospects as the israel regional scene is up there with china for one of the worst, but, shimon has been fighting out of russia recently where he is getting better matchmaking and tougher fights. He rebounded nicely from his contender series loss with a 2nd round ko of former tuf finalist and former ufc fighter william macario. * Edited at 05.17.2022, 12:11 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Middleweight Matej Penaz - 6-0 5 ko. Very impressed with Matej. He is a former glory kickboxer who holds a record of 22-3 (12 kos) and was at one point the #5 ranked middleweight. He held a record of 3-1 in glory winning all three fights by ko. He is 6-0 as a pro but had 3 fights in okatgon’s tournament that are considered exhibition matches, so, he technically is 9-0. Penaz is another person I could see having a huge impact in the ufc. 185 is not a division filled with wrestlers, in fact, quite the opposite actually so if he wins, he should thrive in the divison. His standup is elite and he also trains with Jiri Prochazka on the daily so you know hes learning a lot. SD Dumas - 4 ko 1 sub. SD is really intriguing to me. I think he has all the potential in the world but I do think its a little early for him to be getting the call up (imo), especially because they pitted him against penaz. SD has impressed me in every area of mma. I thought he was just a striker but he actually used his grappling against his toughest opponent yet and won by that. I was a little surprised (and also a little unimpressed if im being honest) to see him take highbaugh to decision but it was good to see because it answered questions i had about skill level and gas tank. On the feet, SD is a very physically imposing 185’er standing at 6”3 and he has some beastly kickboxing skills. You may recognize the name from his viral headkick ko he got at ikon fc a few months ago. SD also went 8-1 as an amatuer. I wish SD didnt get matched up with penaz because i really dont want either guy to lose but win or lose, I think SD will find his way to the ufc sooner rather than later. Very bright future imo Claudio Ribiero - 9-2 8 kos. Reigning Future MMA and Thunder Fight 185 champ. Good ko power and showed that he has good cardio with a 5th round ko in his most recent fight. I will admit, Claudio is one of the few guys I havent heard about before seeing his name signed, but he looks solid from what ive seen. Ozzy Diaz - 7-1 5 ko 2 sub. Ozzy is also an impressive prospect in my eyes because he keeps proving me wrong. He has tons of experience (7-2 amatuer career) and has an insane finishing ability (finished everyone as a pro and all but one person as an amateur) seemingly wherever the fight goes; he has multiple submission and KO wins. He has a few fights for bellator and was the LFA and LXF MW champion. Hes very physically imposing at 6’4 and he picked up a huge win in his most recent outing against the very solid bruno assis (a guy who has only lost to legit fighters) so that was a pretty good indicator of his skill level. Definitely impressed and looking forward to what ozzy is gonna be able to do. Ivan Valenzuela - 8-1 4 ko 3 sub. LFL 185 Champ. He hasnt fought the greatest level of competition but LFL has decently tough matchmaking and he has shown a well rounded skillset, being able to finish wherever the fight goes. Hes matched up against a fellow guy who I also dont know a ton about in claudio ribiero so should be a good fight. Joe Pyfer - 8-2 5 ko 2 sub. 4-1 as an amatuer. Competed on dwcs in 2019 and lost to the not-so-great dustin stolzfus by an injury when he got slammed in a fight where he was already quite a big underdog. Rebounded nicely with a ko of an undefeated, yet poor fighter in CFFC. I must admit, im a little confused as to why they decided to give him another shot as he wasnt that impressive before and hasn’t done anything very impressive since then to make me think he could be a good addition to the ufc. Also, that loss to jhonaven pati has aged so poorly (so has the stoltzfus one too i suppose). Light Heavyweight Oumar Sy - 6-0 3 ko 2 sub. Oumar is a guy who could have a huuuuuuuge impact in the ufc, especially with 205 being so shallow. Oumar is a massive LHW, standing in at 6’5 with an 84 inch reach, almost jon jones esque in stature. With this size also comes a very well rounded skillset. I actually faded him the first time i saw him in ksw (god knows why) and oh boy, did he prove me wrong as he absolutely ran through his opponent. He then impressed me even more with his performance at Ares fc where he beat an undefeated mma factory product. Speaking of undefeated, his opponents have a combined record of 8-0, granted he has fought a few debutants but still good competition nonetheless. Skys the limit for this kid and i think hes got a bright future in the divisionRodolfo Bellato - 8-1 4 sub 4 ko. Started career off 5-0 and lost to his future dwcs opponent, vitor petrino. Hasnt lost since and most recently competed in LFA’s brazil cards where he has looked very dominant. Only fought one person with a losing record too and has looked better has he faces tougher competition. Vitor Petrino - 6-0 6 kos. I really like vitor as a prospect. Hes got insane ko power, finishing everyone hes fought as a pro and amateur (3-1 3 kos). He hasnt fought the greatest level of competition but did beat rodolfo bellato in his debut and most recently destroyed former ufc fighter gadzhimurad antigulov. Trains at CM system but has been spending time at sanford mma which is going to massively help him. Heavyweight Danilo Suzart - 9-1 6 kos. Ares FC HW champ. His only loss is by majority decision so could arguably be undefeated (although i havent seen the fight). Pretty poor level of competition but beat an undefeated HW with a weird style to become champ. Answered questions about his cardio and tdd but overall not sure about skill level because the person he was fighting is ultimately not that great + he hasnt fought great people in the regional scene. At least he has good cardio and tdd which is enough to get you far in the division. Idk where is ceiling is tho, although we have to assume any HW winner will be getting a contract this season. Waldo Cortes-Acosta - 6-0 3 ko 1 sub. LFA HW champion. Definitely the most impressive HW signed this season. Physical specimen, large frame for HW, and has pro boxing experience (6-4). He went 5-1 as an amateur and has fought a very high level of competition as a pro, with his opponents being a combined 43-20. He was supposed to move on to the semi-finals of the RUF: Road to ONE tournament but had to pull out and instead has fought for bellator and LFA in the meantime. He withstood early pressure from the very talented (and someone we will likely see in the ufc in the near future) thomas peterson and koed him. This was a very impressive win in my eyes because thomas carried a long unbeaten streak all the way from his amateur career and had ran through everyone he fought, until he fought Waldo. Waldo did show suspect fight iq when fighting MO Dereese in bellator (this was an interesting fight to say the least) but he still looks very promising, especially as far as HW prospects go. Eduardo Neves - 5-0 5 ko. ONly started mma 3 years ago but neves has been nothing short of impressive since getting into mma. I could be mixing it up with another heavyweight prospect but i believe neves had an extensive kickboxing background (albeit, never at the highest level but experience is experience), even if he hasnt, he has very much looked the aggressive and scary striker thus far in his career. He hasnt left the first round so his cardio is definitely in question, but he is fast, athletic, aggressive, and powerful and I think that could be enough to give him success in the big show. Oh, and hes only 22 which is so ridiculously young for HW, so he has a long career in front of him. Paulo Renato Jr. 10-1 6 ko 1 sub. Only 28 but paulo has been fighting professionally since 2013. He lost his second fight, and hasnt lost since now on a 9 fight winstreak. He stepped away from the sport in 2017 but came back in 2021, winning three fights in three months. His competition is very poor, and he hasnt fought in any major regional promotions so his skill level is questionable but he looks solid from tape. Michael Parkin 4-0 4 ko. 6-0-1 as an amateur with 5 ko and 1 sub. Being on the uk regional scene, parkin has can crushed for the most part, but he has a lot of fights under his belt and clearly is ready for a step up in competition. Hes only left the first round twice and is 1-0-1 in those two fights so that tells me his cardio isnt the best, but thats to be expected at HW. I have a hard time judging skill level of uk prospects, especially those who dont fight in CW because their regional scene has some cans in it, but parkin looks solid. Skill level overall and ceiling is unknown. Caio Machado - 6-1-1 4 ko 1 sub. BFL HW champ. His only loss is to former dwcs fighter and current ONE HW, dustin Joyson, and he hasnt lost since. However, im relatively unimpressed with caio. The canadian regional scene is quite poor and caio has not fought quality opponents whatsoever. In fact, he has two wins over the same guy, lee mein who holds a record of 11-16. Caio should also be fighting at LHW, as he weighs 236 lbs, so hes going to be undersized against all the other HWs. While he is a grappler, which is a rare fighting style for 265, I dont think he will fare well and doesnt look ufc level imo.

posts.author: Fremen
posts.body: I decided to do like a Polish MMA News coverage, I'll post accumulated information in one post, so it doesn't turn out to be spam. One post a couple of days. - Artur Szpilka (boxer) to make his mma debut at KSW 71.- Michał Oleksiejczuk (UFC 4-3-1) to make his Middleweight debut. Oleksiejczuk has not only changed a weight category, but also a club he is training in. Now, he states that he finally feels like training real MMA, sees good future ahead of him. Now, Michał and his brother, Cezary (FEN champion) are training at Ankos MMA in Poznan, Poland. Ankos is next to Red Dragon (Borys Mańkowski, Mateusz Gamrot) one of the best clubs in Poland.source: https://twitter.com/m_turski/status/1527333759829569537?t=GJViUO7P81hCuzxqM9lrEQ&s=19 (Polish MMA podcast/show being casted every week.) * Edited at 05.19.2022, 2:09 PM ET *

posts.body: It appears the best Argentinan prospect, Esteban Ribovics, has either signed with the ufc or dwcs. He posted a picture on isntragram showing signed papers with the ufc logo on them. He would perfectly fit the bill if a dwcs signee; undefeated with all 10 wins by finish. 10-0 5 subs 5 ko. He’s got wins by Superman punch and inverted triangle too. FFC and SFH champion too. Holds one punch ko power and can finish the fight on the ground as well. Very bright prospect and dude is all action. Think he will make a splash as the next big South American prospect. Keep your eyes out for him * Edited at 05.20.2022, 11:02 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Was watching highlights on Twitter for samurai fight house 4 that was on today and I think Argentina has produced yet another prospect, this time in the stacked 135 division, Mauro Mastromarini. He’s now 13-1 with 11 finishes; 8 ko 3 sub and just became the SFH 135 champ. Watching his fight and previous highlights and, holy **** is this kid violent. He hasn’t been fighting the greatest competition but he has been running through who he has fought. He has serious, serious ko power and went 7-0 as an amateur and now 13-1 as a pro and is only 23! He needs to fight better competition before I start calling him a legit prospect but he’s definitely on my radar now. Here’s his highlights below. https://twitter.com/grabaka_hitman/status/1528561266679234560?s=21&t=OhKi9k3F9gtTsKxFI3l9zQ * Edited at 05.22.2022, 11:10 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Seems there’s a new 205 prospect to look out for. Eli aronov just dominated Mariusz ksaskewicz at unified mma 45. Mariusz was likely going to get signed to the ufc with a win and aronov dominated him for every second of the fight. He’s now 6-0 with 5 finishes (2 ko 3 sub). I forget what exactly it was but he was prepping for a Greco Roman wrestling tournament and showed great cardio and wrestling throughout the fight Also, manoel Sousa got a ko in round 2 today at Cffc 109. He finally got pushed to the second round and he was clearly winning the fight but did show some areas where he’s green. He’s so much fun to watch tho; he’s now 9-0 with all finishes and improves to 12-0 overall with 11 finishes. He should either fight for the Cffc belt or go to dwcs imo. I think aronov should get a dwcs opportunity as well. He absolutely dominated a very solid opponent and essentially stole his ufc opportunity

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Looks like we have another 265 dwcs matchup between two very solid prospect. Jimmy Lawson and Kevin Szaflarski Jimmy lawson is 4-1 with 4 ko’s. He lost his debut to Bellator HW Said Sowma and hasn’t lost since, running through his competition. He trains at AKA and was a wreslter at penn state with a record of 39-14. Not as credentialed as a lot of other mma wrestlers but he wrestled for a top program, in a big conference, and yknow, is a HW so a strong wrestling background at a high level is definitely something. Very strong and athletic and he’s working on his striking at aka. Don’t hate the signing one bit but I would have liked to see him get a little more experience, especially because of who his opponent isKevin Szaflarksi is 11-1 with 9 finishes 5 ko 4 sub. This dude is a really intriguing prospect. He had an 8-0 ammy career (winning the IMMAF in 2015) and his only loss came from a leg injury. He’s gigantic for the divison at 6”6 with an 81 inch reach. He can grapple and strike and was the babilon mma and PLMMA champ so he has 5 round cardio. My only issue is his competition isn’t outstanding, but he has a phenomenal track record and again, has only lost from an injury * Edited at 05.28.2022, 1:13 AM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Mansour Barnaoui is only 29 years old, yet he has been fighting since 2011, a time when mma was actually illegal in France. Barnaoui holds a record of 19-4 with 18 finishes; 6 by ko and 12 by submission. He has held multiple world titles for 100% fight (where he fought three times in one night. Then repeating this feat a year later), BAMMA, M-1 Global, and most recently, Road FC where he won their 32 man tournament to win a million dollars and the Road FC 155 belt. Unfortunately, Baranoui has been out of action since winning this belt, but it appears his hunger has once again been satiated as he has now signed with bellator to compete in the 155 division. The Tunisian-born Frenchman carries quite a big frame for 155 standing at 6 feet tall. Unfortunately, I cannot find his reach anywhere but he is very tall for the division. He also has very long legs which allows him to throw knees without telegraphing (see the islam or shamil fight for the best examples of this). His style is fascinating because its so unlike really anyone else’s that I have been able to find. On the feet, he is NEVER fighting more than 2 feet away from you at all times. He doesn't use a whole lot of technique, preferring to slug it out, but hes surprisingly good at finding his target and has improved his striking defense (althought its not that great). His power is insane tho. When he connects, he connects BIG. Where his style gets funky, is in the grappling. For someone with 12 subs and who is a proclaimed grappler, he does not take you down at ALL (I will say, he does do trips a lot in the clinch but almost never does he actually use takedowns). He fights in the clinch, preferring to dirty box and throws tons of knees and jumping knees from the clinch. Its funny, because his takedown defense is quite terrible, but he makes up for it with reversals that are nothing short of phenomenal. I mean this guy reversed islam makhachev at least 5 times when they fought (who does that to islam?) and hes able to do it with his primary grappling weapon, the giggler sweep. This is his trademark move where he isolates a leg, triangles it, and pulls his opponent closer to him, then pushes them off, thus allowing him to get a reversal. Despite being taken down with relative ease, hes never held down for long periods of time, finding his way back up with relative quickness. Again, in the islam fight, he was never held down for longer than 30 seconds on each takedown. Now, there is a blueprint to beating him. As you may notice, while he has only lost to elite fighters, they all have one thing in common and that is they are very high level wrestlers. Now, mansour was able to reverse and get back up, but his inability to avoid the clinch and his disregard for take downs is what cost him in those fights. I dont want it to sound one sided tho, because he was making fights competitive, but if he is to fight a wrestler, that is something to keep in mind (*cough* usman nurmagomedov *cough*). Thats what i love about him though is he truely is one of the only fighters ive seen who never accepts bottom position and is consistently able to get up. When he does get the fight to the ground and hes the one in control, he is very dominant. Because his limbs are so long, his body triangle is very ahrd to escape and hes able to effortlessly maintain it when opponents try to roll out of it. The same goes for his chokes. I believe he has something like 8 or 9 RNC’s on his record and this speaks to how long his arms are because he can sneakily sink it in. In his stretch of fights in ROAD FC, you can see he has improved his striking and learned more technique, going away from primarily fighting in the clinch. What shocks me the most about baranoui is that hes still not even in his prime, and with nearly three years off, I think he is going to come back greatly improved (on whats already an unbelievably impressive fighter). I think he is going to make a splash in bellator.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: was making my picks for EFC 94 and theres actually a few fighters ive got my eye on. the first is Jaco Du Plessis. Yes, that is indeed the brother of Dricus. Jaco actually does not have any combat sports background (funny enough) but he started his amateur career off 4-0 with 3 reported finishes (2ko 1 sub). He couldn't get pro fights because of covid but has restarted his career in the pro circuit last year and won by 2nd round sub. Now with brothers one is almost always worse than the other so where his ceiling is at is tbd. also there is a third du plessis in mma so maybe Jaco is also good lol. Jaco is 26 years old so im interested to see where his career takes him. The main event features the best 135er coming out of South Africa atm in 21 year old Cameron Saaiman. Saaiman is undefeated through pro and amateur, going on 5-0 as an amateur and 4-0 as a pro for a 9 fight win streak overall. Obviously, EFC doesn't have the best matchmaking available, but he hasn't struggled with anyone so far and has finished everyone as a pro with 3 Kos and 1 sub. not sure where his skill level is at either because he hasn't been tested yet, and probably still won't imo because hes fighting an 8-7 guy, but at least hes getting 5 round experience. With 9 fights under his belt and only being 21, im very interested to see where his career is going to take him. Hes pretty big for the division too, standing at 5'9. If he keeps winning, we could see him on dwcs or another big promotion in a couple years. Oh, and both these guys train at the same gym, team CIT * Edited at 05.30.2022, 7:00 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Oktagon 33 happens this Saturday. Funny enough, it starts right around the time ufc ends so if you want to buy the ppv (I think it’s 12 USD) I highly recommend doing so. Oktagon is a very solid promotion with high quality fights but this weekend has some very good fights on it. The prelims are great, with vets like cheik kone and former pfl fighter hatef moeil fighting in the prelims. But where this card really shines is the fantastic main card.For the main card opener, we have David zawada’s first fight since his ufc release. They’re giving him 10-1 prospect, erhan kartal. Kartal is 10-1 with 7 ko’s 1 sub. His only loss came via a knee injury so likely could be undefeated if that hadn’t happened. His level of competition is not great, but if he beats a solid vet like Zawada (who I think should still be in the ufc), then we have a great new prospect to look out for.The next fight is an awesome legends fight between karlos vemola and Michal Pasternak. While I can’t call either prospects, this is going to be an awesome fight between two respective legends in their countries. Vemola fought in the ufc 12 years ago when he was only 24, it wasn’t fully ready for it and was released after a 2-4 stint. Since that however, he has long been one of the best fighters on the European regional scene, going 22-2 (!) since his ufc stint, picking up multiple regional titles including the oktagon MW belt which he held until he had to vacate from injuries. He fights another legend in Michal Pasternak. Michal is a former ONE title challenger and while he hasn’t done anything too impressive, he’s seemed to have hit a career stride at 38, going 7-2 since his loss to Ivan shtyrkov (technically it’s 2-2 but he’s been fighting in wotore which is considered legit mma but is cataloged as exhibition) wotore is polish bare knuckle vale tudo where they fight in a moat(highly recommend looking that up, it’s as great as it sounds). Most recently, Pasternak had a very close split decisjon loss with former dwcs fighter, lukasz soldaski and then crushed a fan to get a win. The next fight is not so intriguing, but it features Christian eckerlin who is a massive star in Germany (forgot to mention, this is Oktagon’s first time in Germany, hence all the German talent). He’s fighting some short notice replacement Who he should beat easily The co-main is a great fight between stefan putz and the older Zawada brother, Martin. Martin is a long time regional vet who’s fought just about everywhere and now he comes to oktagon to fight one of the best fighters in the German regional scene. Putz is unfortunately too old for me to call a prospect (34) but he’s won multiple titles in Germany and was a 2 time challenger for m-1 global 205 belt. He is coming off of a loss to Viktor pesta (which probably would have got him noticed by big orgs) but, he was winning the fight until it was called off by a doctors stoppage. He’s an intriguing 205er because he is 6”5 and has a 78 inch reach. He’s going to look at weight class apart from Zawada. Finally, the main event. Oh man is this one crazy. Ivan buchinger vs Losene Keita. Buchinger is the oktagon double champ and has loooong been one of the best fighters to never go to a major org. He has disgustingly good bjj and has held titles for pretty much every regional promotion you can think of (oktagon,cage warriors, m-1, etc.) and he holds wins over guys like Stevie ray and mansour barnaoui. Standing in front of him however, is someone who I think has potential to be a stud. Losene Kieta is 8-0 with 4 ko’s and is the oktagon 155 IC. He carries an 11 fight winstreak stemming from his amatuer career where he went 3-0. He was born in guinea and left when he was 11 to go to Belgium where he’s been living and training since. He hadn’t fought anyone notable before joining oktagon, but when he did, man did he make a splash. He smacked around the #3 ranked fighter, scoring 4 knockdowns in a dominant first round ko. Then he fought for the interim belt against Ronald paradiser. He did get taken down a lot (which could be concerning against a grappler in buchinger) but he showed a great get up game and phenomenal cardio. Keita can wrestle but where he excels is on the feet. This dude has POWER, especially for someone with only 4 ko’s. He had paradiser knocked down at least 3 times and had him in trouble throughout the 5 round affair. His kickboxing is very clean and he has phenomenal striking defense. Just watching him strike, you can see the skill level. He carries tons of power in his shots but doesnt wind them up, he’s just that strong. He’s got very fast hand speed too and is a very technical striker. He can sometimes overcommit on a shot but the majority of the time he’s composed and tries to pick you apart. One thing he does very well that we don’t see people do, if when he gets a knockdown, he makes his opponent stand back up. This is very smart because he knows he’s winning on the feet and he knows he’s not the most comfortable in the ground, so he doesn’t give his opponent any advantage. This would be a great strategy when he’s fighting buki. Prospects i hype up almost always fall short on big situations, but if he beats buchinger, I can guarantee we will see him in the ufc. I already think he’s great but a win over buchinger, especially a dominant one, means he’s the real deal. Oh, and kieta is only 24, so weather or not he loses, he’s got a great career ahead of him * Edited at 05.31.2022, 8:44 AM ET *
