posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: LFA 134 happens this Friday, and oh man, is it a stacked card. Much like last week, the talent at this show is abundant, and I see lots of potential ufc guys fighting on this card. the first fight I have my eye on is Alden coria vs Joseph Camacho. Coria is a solid prospect in my eyes; until his last fight, he carried a 10 fight unbeaten streak, going 5-0 as a pro and 5-0 as an amateur. He can do a little bit of everything and is very young at only 24. His natural weight class is 135 but he fights at 125 because, as he put it in an interview "once his metabolism slows down and he starts to gain more weight" he will go up to 135 and fight there. His most recent fight was at 135 and he fought a pretty solid prospect and was winning the fight until getting caught up in this absurd grappling sequence where he got tapped out in the third round. Coria trains at a solid team in 4oz fight club and is a bjj black belt with some dangerous finishes. he can scrap on the feet too and im a fan of his striking. He throws lots of kicks to the legs and body and has very good technique on the feet. being a 135er fighting at 125, he carries a massive size advantage being 5'8 (although the 67 inches of reach isn't that great). His opponent is the younger brother of ufc fighter frank camacho, Joseph. Unfortunately, I cant find any tape on Joseph but he carries an undefeated record of 3-0 with 2ko and 1 sub. when I was looking him up, I found a page on his gym where it says hes 6-0 and a purple belt in bjj. Im going to assume those extra three fights were some suspect fights in Guam that didnt get reported but yeah. I have this weird theory that is somewhat proven where one brother is always better than the other so maybe we see a new camacho to look out for. I dont know his age either but he hasn't fought since 2020 so he has likely been working on himself during these 2 years off. The next fight I have my eye on is jakori savage vs Taylor escamila. this fight is gonna be awesome; two former collegiate atheltes fighting one another. This is especially interesting because, with the lack of HWs in the ufc, we could see the winner make it to the big leagues before long. Escamilia is 1-0 with 1 ko and a 2-0 ammy career. Most of the information on his wrestling career is hidden by paywalls (**** you Fremont tribune!) but he was a wrestler for university of Nebraska-Omaha (a D2 school) and won three straight titles with them. He was also an all American there in 2011. However, he has been inconsistent throughout his mma career, fighting twice as an ammy through 2012-13 and his only pro fight was in 2019 in one Alaska FC 🤢. He could be good tho but yknow, Alaska fc lol. His opponent, Jakori Savage is someone I think has potential, but is very very green. However, the talent is definitely there, just needs experience and refining. Jakori is an incredible athlete as he was an OL for LSU (for my non-american friends, LSU is in the top 3 best American football colleges in the country) so he was very good at his sport and very athletic. Hes also gigantic, standing at 6"6 with an 86 inch reach! I mean that build alone is just insane. He lost his amateur debut and then rattled off 4 straight wins as an amateur and 1 as a pro, winning all by ko. Hes got power and loves to throw elbows and knees in the clinch. His gas tank doesn't look great but he does have 2nd and 3rd round ko wins as an ammy (yes, ik the rounds are different but still, thats longer than 5 minutes) his tdd is also interesting to me. He got taken down a few times in his lfa debut, but he was able to reverse position (likely because of his pure strength, size, and athleticism) and even managed to reverse his opponent while he was fully mounted on jakori, so that is definitely impressive. It will be interesting to see how he does with the takedowns from a collegiate wrestler but I think he will win the fight. Very intriguing fighter imo, and I hope he has success so we see him in the ufc one day. The next fight im looking at is the co main event, between Milko Tucto and dwcs alum, Olivier Murad. Murad is a pretty solid prospect but he does need some work on his overall game. He carries a 13 fight winsrtreak overall, going 7-0 as an amateur (picking up some titles along the way) and 6-0 as a pro. He has won multiple regional titles, including the titan fc 145 championship where he upset the very solid Muhammadjon naimov in a 5 round battle. He came on dwcs and really didnt impress me all that much ( I actually thought he lost), maybe it was nerves, maybe his opponent was just better than him, or maybe hes just actually not that good (assuming one of the former two). He looked hesitant and even when he got a knockdown, he didnt really capitalize and also got outwrestled for a lot of the fight. He can do a little bit of everything but where he excels the most is in the striking. Hes kind of low volume but hes got good power and throws a lot of unorthodox stuff like combos with different types of elbows. His opponent is Milko Tucto, someone I haven't really heard of but will provide a solid test for Olivier. He may have an ugly 5-3 record, but he went 8-1 as an amateur (only loss by split decision) and his three losses as a pro have came to Ivanovich chalo (idk who this is but hes got an okay record), Erick Gonzalez (horrible loss but for this level of competition, not bad), and William Starks (another loss that has aged very poorly, but, despite Starks very poor performance, I still think hes a great prospect). I expect murad to win this pretty easily, but, tucto may give him a tougher fight than it looks on paper. Depending on how easy he makes it look, id say we see murad get another dwcs opportunity within a year, just needs 1-3 wins, depending on how he looks. Now, the main event. Man this is gonna be such a great fight between two solid prospects. Like murad, I was quite unimpressed with gaufarov, but, hes still a solid fighter, just not so sure hes gonna be making it to the ufc anytime soon. hes 17-4 with 15 finishes (8 ko 7 sub) and has a myriad of experience fighting in ONE, ACA, and UAE warriors. Hes fought (and beaten) a very high level of competition and pairs this with being a two time world samba champion. He has a very well rounded skillset; on the feet he carries good power and loves to throw spinning kicks, and in the grappling hes got good sambo, good reversals, and a sneaky submission game. However, he came to dwcs and defied all expectations; I mean I guess anheliger isn't THAT bad but hes not great and ruin really **** the bed against him. I dont understand if he just folded because he wasn't on the sauce (even though he tested positive after the fight lol) or if he too choked under the spotlight. He could come into this fight an look great, as dwcs alum have a knack for impressing me (see Maria silva or Justin burnlinson) but idk. My one biggest qualm with him is his tdd. For someone with an impressive samb0 background, he gets taken down and controlled a LOT. Like a lot. IN most of his one fights, his opponents get him down with ease and he can get back up, but will sometimes just stay on his back. Thats why I think this is the perfect matchup for Sousa. Sousa is 15-1 with 12 finishes (2 ko 10 sub) and man is this guy impressive. On the feet, he doesn't have much to offer, as hes low volume and likes to calculate his shots, but where he excels is on the ground. Hes got dominant top control and very good wrestling and bjj. I think we will see some great scrambles from these two. Sousa has been on a long layoff, but is inly 28 so im assuming hes developed some good skills since then. He too has a myriad of experience although gaufarov has fought better competition. Sousa was the Future FC 135 champ and would have been the cffc 135 champ (beating a very solid fighter in Alexander keshtov) but he did miss weight. Overall, I think Sousa will win this fight because he has the better takedowns and top control and the better gas tank. Should be an insane fight and id bet the winner goes to the ufc (not so sure in gaufarovs case but I feel Sousa will pretty much be a shoe in with a win here)

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Tahir abdullaev just won at amc fight nights in an absolute war. It was fun and he showed a lot of his varied skilleset, but, he also showed a bunch of holes like gassing out in the third (tbf I do not know how he didn’t ko his opponent; dude had one of the best chins I’ve ever seen), swinging wild shots, and being quite hittable. However, it was an absolute brawl and he did show some great reversals and some deadly striking. I don’t think it’s a ufc worthy fight but he’s 25, 13-1, and now on a 7 fight win streak. He has now beaten two solid opponents in a row and I’m sure the ufc has had their eye on him since uae warriors 24, where Dana was in attendance. We may see him in the ufc soon

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise

I would say we likely see him on dwcs, but it wouldn’t surprise me if he goes to an org like ksw or ACA instead because of visa issues. posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Brunno Ferriera - 7-0 4 ko 3 sub. I actually dont know a ton about brunno but he carries a nice undefeated record finishing everybody hes fought. Hes got a well rounded skillset and has fought relatively good competition throughout his career, with a record of 51-30-1 Roybert Echeverria - 7-0 4 ko 1 sub. 6-3 ammy record with 1 ko 4 sub. Ive known about Roybert for a little bit. hes from goat shed which is notorious for producing can crushers, which he has done his whole career. The best opponent hes fought, wascar cruz, he won in what was a pretty **** robbery. Wascar is okay but hes a journeyman, prospects should run through him. Hes exciting but he has tons of work to do. Not expecting too much from him but, 125 needs prospects so he has to do. Dont expect him to win a contract unless he faces a can or gets an early ko. Leon Aliu - 10-1 6 ko 4 sub. So i was exaggerating a little bit when I said aliu may have punched his ticket to dwcs in his last fight, but, I turned out to be right. Hes a bit old for a prospect at 32, but hes got good experience and finished everyone hes fought. He suffered his first loss in 2018, took three years off (I think because of injuries and then the pandemic), and came back in 2021 going 2-0. In his most recent fight, he made his cage warriors debut 2 weeks ago and absolutely flatlined James hendin. Hes likely gonna fight brunno and im excited to see what he can do. looks promising but the age is a little concerning. Marnic Mann - 5-0 3 ko 1 sub. 4-0 ammy record 1 ko 2 sub. So Mann probably would have been signed during the female free agency fire sale a few weeks ago, but, she kind of cruised in a fight where she should have absolutely demolished her last opponent. She is promising but I didnt like to see that last fight. she is a grappler who has good subs and nasty GnP. David Bollea - 7-2 7 subs. Yeah this one is just strange. dude is 41 and has can crushed. he retired in 2012 on a 2 fight losing streak and came back in 2021, rattling off 4 straight first round finishes. I mean I just dont understand this at all and I can only assume he impressed dana (probably with his attitude) enough to give him a shot. Hes gonna get his ass kicked and I can only assume hes gonna be a stepping stone for a hot prospect. Or a bum who puts him on their highlight reel Bruna Brasil - 7-2-1 2 ko 2 sub. I really like this signing. Bruna started her career off 1-2-1, losing to ariane carnelossi in her debut and Jessica delboni; two tough fights for early in your career. She also had a pro boxing bout where she lost to an undefeated boxer. Since this rough start, she's now on a 6 fight win streak, and in her most recent fight, she dominated an undefeated prospect. And I mean dominated, she looked flawless everywhere. Ikram Aliskerov - 12-1 4 ko 4 sub. this is my favorite signing out of the new additions. Ikram is really ******* good. you may recognize the name from his brutal ko loss to khamzat chimaev, which is nothing to scoff at. That also happens to be his only loss. He spent the majority of his career in Brave CF, and most recently dominated nah-shon burell in Eagle FC (cool to see they started signing eagle fc guys). Besides being a phenomenal mma fighter, Ikram has a very very high level combat sambo game. Now sure, all these dagestanis do, but Ikram is seriously credentialed; he is a 4 time combat sambo world champ, gold medaling 7 times. He has held gold in 2016 FIAS worlds, 2012 WCSF worlds, 2014 and 2016 FIAS World Cup, 2017 and 2012 European championships, and 2013 Russia combat sambo championship (also silver medaling in 2017). If you weren't convinced his sambo is filthy, the dude has beaten Yaroslavl amosov, Murad Kerimov (I dont know sambo like that but apparently this guy is nasty at the sport), and top prospect Raimond Magomedaliev. Oh, and hes only 29; this kid is really ******* good and doesn't even need dwcs imo. * Edited at 06.12.2022, 5:41 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Haha I called it, Austen lane getting his dwcs opportunity. He’s fighting Richard Jacobi. Jacobi is 5-0-1 with 3 ko’s. He hasn’t fought the best competition but made his lfa debut against an undefeated fighter a few months ago, ending in a split draw. Now, that’s not the greatest thing to see, and unfortunately I couldn’t watch the fight because it was on the lfa prelims, but I’m pretty sure he should have won the fight considering the ufc signed him to dwcs off of a draw. Not sure what to expect considering his last fight and his level of competition not being so greatAusten Lane is 11-3 with 10 ko’s and 1 sub. Now, you guys might remember me bringing his name up as somebody who I was confused hasn’t been signed to dwcs, but now he is. You also may remember his name as he is an ex-NFL player who got starched by Greg hardy in his 4th pro fight on dwcs. Now, Austen has improved a ton, becoming the fury fc HW champion. Fury doesn’t offer great competition and Austen is a bit older at 34 (luckily that’s pretty young for 265), but there’s a lot I like out of him. His biggest hole is his tdd, but that won’t be an issue against a striker. He is massive for the division at 6”6 with an 80 inch reach and is very athletic and has great cardio for the divison, as shown in his title fight. He fought former ufc fighter who was on the rebound in Juan adams, and he was losing a fairly one sided fight, before adams gassed in the 4th and he got the ko. Again, not good to see him get mauled like that but his cardio is quite impressive for the big guy that he is. Then, his title defense was odd because he won from a checked leg kick that broke his opponents leg. Two odd endings to fights but still, I am impressed with the improvements he’s made. Still not sure about his celing (probably middle tier HW tbh; if he wins that is lol) but I am excited to see what he can do. * Edited at 06.13.2022, 1:00 PM ET *

He’s somebody I thought the ufc would sign for dwcs so this was shocking to see Bellator has him Kamil Oniszsczuk - 9-1 ACA and babilon mma vet. Picked up two great ko’s most recently in ACA Otto Rodrigues - 13-1 had an appearance on LFA 112 where he beat a 19-2 guy. Also a former jungle fight 145 champ. Dude looked very solid so I was shocked the ufc didn’t offer himOleg Popov - this one really shocked me because Popov is one of the best guys not in the ufc. Idk how Bellator got him or why they haven’t used him yet (probably visa issues like it is with most of these guys) but he will be good once they debut him; even tho they’re just gonna feed him cans Sarvadzhon khamidov - 13-0 fought in many good regional promotions like amc fight nights and UAE warriors. Even fought on the lookin for a fight card but didn’t get offered. This dude is pretty good and fights at 135. Hood addition for Bellator but I’m surprised ufc didn’t jump on it

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Darrius flowers, amirlan gogoladze, Josh Wang Kim, and Cameron saaimon all added to dwcs roster. Love all of these signings, especially JWK he’s my favorite prospect atm

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Darius flowers is 11-5 with 7 ko’s and 1 submission. On paper he looks like a terrible regional guy (with quite the ugly boxing record too) but he got this signing because he gained notoriety for picking up 3 wins in 20 days, with two of them being ko wins over solid guys in LFA. We will have to see if his luck continues Amirlan gogoladze is 14-2 with 7 ko and 3 sub. I recently saw him fight on gfc 16, where he won in a dominant, first round ko, to become the GFC MW champ. Amirlan has a lot of experience, and trains out of the very underrated (imo) UFD gym in Germany. He carries a ton of power and has lots of high level experience in UAE warriors and M-1 Cameron Saaiman is 5-0 with 3 ko 1 sub. He train at team CIT with Dricus du plessis and despite only being 21, He brings a 10 fight winstreak with 5 wins as an amatuer and 5 as a pro. He just recently became the efc bantamweight champion, proving he can go five rounds and going the distnce for the first time in his career. He is really young, but the potential is there. If he doesn’t win this season, I’m sure we will see him back eventually. Josh Wang-Kim is 5-1 with 2 ko’s and 2 sub. You may have heard me mention him before, as he is my favorite e prospect out there. His style is super entertaining; he fights in a karate stance similar to wonderboy, and has a diverse striking arsenal. He also has shown solid wrestling and sick submission game. His last fight was odd; he has a weird jon Jones esque situatuon where he threw elbows that were illegal (I think they may have been) and then his opponent tried to punch JWK after the bell, which should have been a dq on his part. It was very weird but whatever, he’s still very good imo

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: this week is just fantastic for the up and coming prospects. Its almost like its the theme for all promotions this week, even bellator and ufc is chock full of their best up and comers. This week, there are 5 promotions putting on shows with a phenomenal array of prospects; many of whom I think we will see in the ufc/bellator/pfl/one within 1-3 years (for many, this is their breakthrough fight that will get them signed somewhere). Unfortunately, a lot of the cards got beaten up with fight cancellations but luckily, the best prospects remain on all the cards Levels Fight League 4 - Sunday First, we have the pro debut of Cedric manhoef. Yes, that is indeed a relative of Melvin manhoef, his cousin in fact. Cedric is a kickboxer with a record of 53-10 and he was ranked in the top 10 for active middleweights when he still fought (hes been gone since 2019 likely prepping for mma). Unfortunately, LFL gave him a grappler with amateur experience (5-3) but, giving him the exact opposite fighting style will help give him experience. Will be interesting to see how he does. Anmari diedrick welcomes Rick Kuiper back to the cage for the first time since 2016. Diedrick is someone I had the pleasure of watching at ares fc 4 and he really impressed me beating a solid 2-0 dagi from the MMA factory. Hes young and was a former glory kickboxer with a record of 19-9; he went 1-2 in glory. He fights rick kuipers in what should be an easy fight for anmari. Kuipers hasn't fought since 2016 and is also a former Muay Thai fighter (cant find info on his fights sadly) from way back in the day, so he isn't going to wrestle anmari and will play into his fight style. This is a good fight to build anmari and I think he should pass with flying colors Next we have Felipe Micheletti who is making his mma debut. Felipe is a former kickboxer with a record of 16-9. its not very impressive but, he has been ranked in the HW top 10 since 2018 and before he left the sport, was ranked #6 in the world. He also held an amateur kickboxing record of 30-7. Again, the record may be ugly but hes only losing to elite guys like Alex pereira, Luis Tavares, Stephane Susperrigri, and Vladimir mine to name a few. He fights Virgil Augen who is a respectable 2-0 with imaaf experience. Now, we have Manuel Gaxja fighting for the LFL 155 strap. You may recognize the name as he was slated to be on dwcs last year, but couldn't compete because of visa issues. I honestly think that was a blessing in disguise because he has a horribly padded record so more epxirence will help him. he is 7-0 with 2 ko and 3 sub. Hes fighting his toughest test in 4-2 Akonne Wanliss (7-1 ammy record as well). Again, he has seriously can crushed, but from the tape ive seen hes actually quite impressive, just needs to fight someone with a pulse. He has fantastic kicks that he throws with tons of power and can also grapple. Id bet he goes to dwcs with a. win here. In the main event we have former ufc 185er, Tom Breese, defending his LFL 185 strap against Ahmed Sami. Breese was in the ufc from 2015-2021 until he was unfortunately (and unfairly) cut from the ufc. I mean, he was a pullout god but it still wasn't that justified considering his last fight in the ufc was a loss to a ranked opponent. Breese is a finisher and has really good grappling. Id assume a win here puts him in prime position to take a short notice fight to get back into the ufc, or at worst a look from bellator or pfl. I think he would fit perfectly into all 3 promotions. Fury FC 65 - Sunday sadly, this event had two of its best fights (lozoya fight and Shelton fight) fall through, but hopefully they can find replacements. With the cancellations this is still a pretty solid show, especially for fury fc, who usually has bums fighting. First, we have Isaiah Diggs making his pro debut. Diggs was a wrestler for CSU-pueblo, I sadly cannot find anything else about his wrestling background other then he wrestled there. I am interested in him because he had a great ammy career that included a fight in LFA. he went 6-0 with 3 Kos and his opponents had a combined record of 13-3. he fights the debuting Pena allamov In the co-main event we have themba gorimbo making in U.S. debut against Julio Rodrigues. Themba was a very unexpected signing imo but I like it a lot. Themba astarted out at 155, and after an unsuccessful title challenge, he moved up to 170 where he became the EFC champ. He then vacated the belt where he went to UAE warriors and lost to the very solid Handesson ferriera. Themba is a well rounded fighter and im a big fan of his jab and his 1-2. they are thrown without much wind up or telegraphing and they are very effective. He also has pretty impressive grappling, with slick back takes and a great triangle choke because of his long legs. One thing I dont like about him is his head movement is not great, and leads to him getting clipped occasionally. He fights a tough vet and if he gets the win, id expect him to be on dwcs. If he doesn't impress the UFC, Id expect him to fight for the fury fc 170 belt and then get a dwcs opportunity with a winThe main event is a great fight, and in terms of fury FC, a fantastic fight. You have two dwcs vets fighting for the fury fc 135 belt in Joshua weems and mo miller. Mo is very obviously the guy they want to win but I think if either guy gets a win, they will get to the ufc; Im not sure if either would have to go through dwcs again tho. Weems is a solid regional guy with great grappling. However, he had a very unimpressive showing on dwcs, losing to the very bad fernie garcia in a first round ko. Since this, he fought and won the B2 135 title by first round sub. Mo is a top prospect for 135, having a very high level wrestling game. He had a dominant performance on dwcs but dana wasn't impressed. He was likely gonna get another dwcs shot with a win, but he lost his post dwcs fight in a huge submission upset to Jose johnson. He was winning the fight easily and then gassed himself out and got subbed. he has rebounded with a first round ko. Mo is quite good and hes also fighting a pretty chinny guy so barring a freak upset, I think we will see mo in the ufc quite soon. Cage Warriors 140 - Saturday First fight i have my eyes on is frederico pascuali vs harry hardwick. Pascuali is 7-0 with 4 ko and 2 sub. He carries a 10 fight win streak overall (3-0 ammy) and he has been looking great on the regional scene. He made his CW debut after a run in venator and destroyed paul mcbain as a big underdog. He fights harry hardwick, brother of 155 title challenger, george hardwick. Harry is 8-3 with 1 ko and 2 subs. He has experience in bellator and cw, where he holds a 2-0 record in the promotion. He is 5-1 in his last 6 with his only loss coming against tuf 27 vet, richie smullen. He isnt as exciting as his brother, but he is definitely above average. Im not sure who wins but the winner is setting themselves up nicely for a dwcs opportunity (espeically hardwick, as having two brothers on dwcs (pending his brother winning the title) is something the ufc likes to do). IN the co main event we have 2 undefeated prospects fighting one another in, Caolan Loughrane and Festus Ahorlu. Both guys are 5-0 and both have not fought great competition. Caolan in fact, has fought both reece street and will cairns, so just a tremendously padded record. His opponents record as a pro is 5-75… He did have a 7-2 ammy career fighting decent guys so that somewhat makes up for it. He did also look pretty solid in his last fight. Festus on the other hand, has fought far better comp, with his last two opponents being 8-1 and 6-2 respectively. He is making his CW debut and carries a 10 fight winstreak stemming from the ammys (holding a 7-1 record there). My pick is festus but i think we will have to keep our eyes out for the winner of this bout. Not sure any major org has their eyes on either guy, but with both being the co main and undefeated, i think this is a breakout fight for both. The main event for CW 140 is just fantastic. You have a former ufc vet in rhys mckee vs a dwcs vet in Justin Burlinson. Both guys have recovered nicely from their setbacks and are fighting for the 170 strap. I would say the winner goes straight up to the ufc but we will have to see. Rhys is 11-4-1 with 8 kos and 3 subs. He had quite the unfortunate ufc run, being fed to khamzat chimaev and then alex morono (who set a record for significant strikes in a three round fight). Rhys wasnt ready but that doesnt mean hes bad. He is a brawler and he does get hit a lot (but so does burlinson so thats okay) but he can take a punch to give a punch. He is fighting top prospect Justin Burlinson. Burlinson was highly regarded coming into dwcs, holding a 17-1 ammy record and a 6-0 pro record (at the time; now 7-1 with 2 ko and 5 sub). He fought yohan lainesse and got slept with a nasty left hook. Like rhys, burlinson is very hittable but man can he brawl. His fight against skibinski is one of my fav fights this year; they just went to war and burlinson started off cold, getting overwhelmed by skibinskis striking, but he started to work the body which eventually paid dividends and he gassed out skibinski and got a sub once he hurt him. He has a nasty ground game to go along with his hands. I really like burlinson but i wont be mad at whoever wins.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: UWC 35 - Friday Much like fury fc, UWC has good prospects, but low level of competition. However, this event is pretty stacked with some fighters i really like. First, we have alan martinez vs roberto luna. These guys are 3-1 and 4-0 respectively. This is a good fight to judge who is a good prospect as both guys have built solid records, but havent fought anyone (mostly 0-0 guys). Luna struggled as an amatuer, going 6-3 but he has built up a nice pro record. Martinez is 4-0 and also went 3-0 as an amatuer (one fight isnt reported). He hasnt fought anyone with a pulse either but is clearly skilled enough to win fights so this will be a good test. His nickname is super glue because he is very hard to get off once he takes you down The next fight im looking at is the return of daniel barez. Dani Barez is 13-5 with 5 kos and 5 subs. Barez was in a razor close fight (that i thought he won) against a guy i rate pretty highly in carlos hernandez. Barez is older for a prospect at 33 but he really impressed me on dwcs. He didnt land the greatest shots, but his grappling and tdd really impressed me. Hes being given yet another can (got fed one earlier this year) so this should be an easy win for him. I wish they would give him a tougher fight but maybe this is all he needs to get back to dwcs. I wouldnt be surprised if he gets a short notice call up either. The main event features the brother of dwcs competitor andres luna martinelli. His brother is Adrian Luna martinelli. He went 3-0 as an amatuer and is 9-1 as a pro with 1 ko and 3 subs. Hes not nearly as good as his brother, but he is the current uwc 135 champ. The reason he didnt get signed to compete is because he is just getting spoon fed cans. Like really bad cans (fighting another one this time around) so its hard to judge his skill level. Like his brother, he has very creative bjj and even picked up a gogoplata in his last fight. Hes making another title defense against a bum so with a win, i dont think hes gonna get a dwcs shot, but hopefully they give him a quality opponent next time, and then maybe we will see him on dwcs. Solid guy tho just need him to be tested Ares FC 7 - Saturday Now this is almost as good an event as the ufc card this week. Ares is a great promotion but this card is absolutely phenomenal. There are at least 5 people i can see fighting in the ufc on this card and they have so many great matchups and prospects fighting. First, we have melissa dixon. Now, i actually think her opponent is 1. The better fighter and 2. Winning the fight, but rizlen is old so i cant call her a prospect. If dixons beats her however, shes someone im gonna be looking out for then. Dixon is 3-0 with 2 kos, also holding a 5-4 ammy record with imaaf experience. This is a massive step up and she is taking it on short notice, but if she beats rizlen (who i think is a quality opponent), then she is someone to look out for Next, we have more women prospects, but this time they are actually promising + i know about them. Anastasia fedofanova vs ewelina wozniak. Wozniak is 6-1 2 ko and 2 sub. She held a 7-2 ammy record as well. She is coming off of a loss to maria silva in a fight where she got beat up. Now, i think silva is just really good but it does make me rethink ewelina’s skill level. She looks very promising, but is also not exactly being given a favorable matchup here. Fedofanova is a really intriguing prospect imo. Shes 8-1 with 4 kos and 2 subs. She has fought solid comp, with a record of 17-10 but her last two opponents have both been undefeated (one being yuila ostroverkova who i also rate as a solid prospect). Fedofanova is coming off of an insane reverse triangle choke and is looking to make another statement. I think the winner of this will get a dwcs opportunity; both are very solid and well rounded women. Next, we have amin Ayoub fighting damien lapilus. Ayoub is 16-5 with 2 ko and 8 subs. He is the former brave CF 155 champ and is making his ares fc debut. Im not as excited for this fight as the other ones because ayoub is still under brave contract (fights there next month too) but, hes still a solid prospect. He has a ton of experience and is a great grappler. Hs fighting damien laplius who isnt great, but he has a lot of experience and can push ayoub. I hope we see ayoub in a major rog eventually but he is currently with brave so im just gonna watch this one to enjoy it. The next fight is akhmed salamov vs jose galindo. Salamov is 4-1 with 4 subs and trains out of the mma factory. Much like his teammate, lazy king (more on him later), salamov has a crazy high level grappling game. He has a mix of dagestan wrestling mixed in with creative bjj. His last fight was a wild scrambling battle with tons of reversals and momentum switches, where he won by 3rd round kneebar. He fights the undefeated jose galindo. I dont know much about him, but i know he is a striker with a good record fighting in the peruvian regional scene. This is for the ares fc 185 belt and i think salamov will get signed to dwcs with a win here. He would be a good fit for 185 considering there arent many good grapplers in teh division The next fight im looking at is william gomis vs renan barao. Gomis is 9-2 with 5 kos and 1 sub. Gomis is one of my favorite oroispects because nobody knows him, yet he is tremendously skilled. He has beaten solid guys like tobias harila and his only two losses were in his first 3 fights and were against morgan charriere and salahdine parnasse. He is very good everywhere and has shown some great fight iq in the cage so far. He is getting a freebie win against the ghost of renan barao, so expect a highlight reel, dominant performance from him. Im pretty sure he will get outright signed to the ufc with a win here, if they value him as much as i do. He is very good. Next is a fight i originally thought Luis henrique was gonna win, but after hearing slim trabelsi was an olympic wrestler, not so much confidence anymore. Slim is 3-0 with 2 kos and as i mentioned, used to wrestle at the olympic level.I first caught wind of him in LFL 2, where he mauled solid prospect john winter. Slim is green, but his wrestling is high level and we know thats in short supply at 265 so that makes him a hot commodity. A win over a ufc vet is huge for the career too. Henrqiue was never good in the ufc, and he still isnt good, but he has been on a tour since leaving the ufc, fighting the top 265 prospects like kornilov (more on him later) and maslov, so he will provide a good test for slim. With a win, i think slim will be a shoe in for dwcs. The main event stars one of the top prospects worldwide in lazy king. This is his first defense of the ares 170 belt and he fights french mma legend, karl ammousou. Ammousou is the biggest name lazy king has fought and holds a record of 27-9 with loads of experience being the CW champ and former bellator fighter (where he challenged for a title). Lazy King is a phenomenal prospect. He has super high level grappling and has some of the most creative bjj set ups ive seen. He is so much fun to watch and with a win here, he is an instant shoe in for the ufc imo. I think he could easily debut in the ufc top 15 (and just might). He is also the former brave cf 170 champ. The fight i care about the most on this card (yes, more than the lazy king one) is shamil Gadzhiev vs Kirill Kornilov. This fight is sooooo good and the winner of this will be established as an elite 265 prospect. I mean these two guys are very very good and the winner will get signed to the ufc straight up as well. Gadzhiev is yet another 265er with high level wrestling. He is from dagestan so you lready know how he gets down. He is 7-0 with 5 kos and 2 subs. He also holds a 9-2 ammy record that includes winning gold at the 2019 immaf. His opponent, kirill kornilov is, imo, an elite fighter. He is 13-0-1 and is a former glory kickboxer with a record of 14-3. He mixes in the kickboxing beautifully into mma. He has some holes and his tdd is going to be tested here, but if he passes i have no doubt he could be ranked in the ufc. His jab is beautiful and he is nasty in the clinch. He really does have a high level striking game and is also gigantic for the division at 6’5. His level of competition is insane too, with a combined record of 132-56 and holds wins over 3 former ufc vets. Really excited for this one, and its my most anticipated regional fight this week

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Rogue MMA 3 - Friday Only one interesting fight to me on this card and it involves former ufc fighter Isi Fitekfu fighting for the RMMA 185 strap. Isi was signed to the ufc to be a backup for a fight (cant remember what) and then was scheduled to fight dalcha lungiambula but isi pulled out which led to him being cut by the ufc. He hasnt fought since 2019 and hes being fed a can for a win here, but i wonder if the ufc will think about resigning him once he wins. We saw a similar situation with dean barry where he got a win and was subsequently resigned. Rizin 36 - Saturday So here we actually have not one, but two prospects fighting one another in rizins main event this weekend. Well, im not sure we can really call kai asakura a prospect considering hes one of the best 135ers not signed to a major org (actually idk if rizin is considered major or not but you guys know what i mean by “major”) but, hes not int he ufc so im gonna refer to him as such. He has been a rizin mainstay for the majority of his career, notching wins over guys like kyoji horiguchi, manel kape, alatengheili, and hiromasa ogikubo. Now, he is coming off of a huge upset loss against the very good (and a guy who i wish the ufc signed off of TUF 24) hiromasa ogikubo, but i think with a win here he may get signed to the ufc, heres why; a few months ago, kai was training with manel kape and merab while having multiple meetings with dana white. I think this may mean kai to the ufc will be sooner than later. Now, he does have to get through his opponent first, the undefeated ji yong jang. Jang is 4-0 as a pro and 5-0 as an amatuer. Hes only got 1 ko and 1 sub, but hes beaten relatively good comp for how green he is, with a combined record of 8-1. Hes getting a gigantic step up in kai, who i think could debut in the ufc top 15, but if he somehow pulls off this upset, we will have a new prospect to look out for. Kai likely wins this with ease however. Fury FC 66 - Sunday The fighter i have my eyes on is ½ of the main event in Gilbert Nakatani. He is 4-0 with 2 ko and 2 subs. He also had an ammy career of 10-2. He is also a flyweight which helps. He has fought pretty bad competition but is getting a solid step up in gianni vasquez. Vasquez is 7-4 and has some hype as he is coming off of a brutal ko win where he flatlined his opponent with a standing elbow. If vasquez gets a win here, we may see him on contender series. BFL 73 - Thursday The prospect i have my eyes on here is Navid Zanageh. So this one is actually fascinating to me because nobody knows about this kid and hes very under the radar. So why am i interested in a guy who 1. Nobody knows about and 2. Has 0 mma experience at any level? Well my friends, this is because Navid is a former member of Iran’s U23 wrestling team. Not only that, he has won gold at the Asian Games, a Silver Medal at the 2019 U23 Asian games, and a bronze at 2018 worlds. Hes only 25 years as well, so he hasnt even hit his athletic prime. Clearly, BFL thinks very highly of him because they are throwing him right into a 5 round title fight, against a guy with 13 fights combined pro and ammy (5-3 pro, 4-0 ammy). Now, obviously hes not fighting a good guy, but going from 0 experience in mma at any level, to fighting for a title against a guy with lots of experience is a pretty tough test for a debut. If he wins, he is definitely someone to look out for, especially because 205 is so thin. UAE Warriors 31 - Sunday Unfortunately, this event is not really stacked with prospects like its saturday (more on that one later), but the one fight i am interested in has a prospect i personally like a lot His name is Yousri Belgaroui and he is 3-1 with 2 kos. He is a former glory kickboxer with a 27-7 record. He was ranked in the top 10 while he was active and got as high as the number 2 middleweight in the world at one point. His best win was jason wilnis but he did go to a split decision with adesanya. He also fought alex pereira twice and donovan wisse so he has fought some animals in kickboxing. In mma, he has looked pretty damn good so far. His only loss was a split decision to a 6-1 guy. He is fighting 4-0 Mohammad Oseli. Oseli is also a striker so this fight should be really exciting.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: UAE Warriors 30 - Saturday Now this card is ******* stacked. There are a lot of prospects on this card. Some i really like and others look solid, but some need to develop more. First, we have Jaqueline Cavalcanti. You may recognize the name from PFL challenger series this year. She is 2-1 but she had a very close fight on PFL that she arguably could have won. She is fighting the literal definition of a can, but since shes only 2-1 ill let it slide cuz she needs exprerience. Im not really all that interested in her, but being a 145er we could see her in the big leagues with only a few wins. Next, we have a guy who ive seen at lot of and am definitely a fan of, but i need to see a little more of him to fully judge his ceiling. His name is samuel bark and he is 3-1 with 2 kos and has a muay thai background. I cant find a ton on his career, but i do know he was ranked in the top 10 as a competitor and was champ in a few stadiums in thailand. He has can crushed so far, but had a close decision to a prospect i rate in Alexander Loof. He fights Alexandru Chitorian who is 4-1. I dont know much about him, but he is coming off of a loss to do geoym lee, who isnt very great. He is still the second best person Bark has fought so this will be a good test. KB Bullhar isnt someone i really care about all that much, nor do i think hes really that good, but he is solid for the regional scene, is a former ufc fighter, and his winning fights so with a win here we could see him get closer to the opportunity, maybe even put himself in position for a short notice replacement or contender series shot (yes ik he was former ufc but he got into the ufc from short notice when he was supposed to be on contender series). The one good thing about him is he is young and big for the division so he does have some potential, but needs a lot of improvements. Hes 3-1 since his ufc departure and his only loss was via petr yan illegal knee against current pfl signee, tarek sulieman. He fights Juscelino Ferriera who is 11-3 and coming off of a loss to denis tiuliulin, so that speaks to his skill level. Hes still a solid opponent for KB. The next prospect im looking at is Gabriel Alves Braga. Braga is 7-0 and has been killing it on LFA recently so its good to see him on a bigger stage. He is 7-0 with 4 kos. He has been doing really well in LFA, fighting on their brazilian cards. What really stands out to me is his level of comp. His opponents are a combined 34-14, but one opponent had 10 of those losses. That was his last opponent but they were a short notice replacement (probably why he hasnt gotten a dwcs look yet, as he was slated to fight Jose Delano, who is very good btu also pulled out of that). He fights Faeez Jacobs who has an ugly record, but is the former EFC 135 champ and has a well rounded skillset. A win here could get braga a dwcs look i think. The 145 champ is who i have my eyes on next. Ali Alqaisi is 12-5 with 2 ko and 3 subs. He is a former ufc fighter, who went 0-2 but had two close and pretty exciting fights. He is a decisionator, but hes been decently entertaining. Since his ufc departure, he has been killing it, going 4-0 and picking up the Titan FC and UAE warriors 145 belt (which he is defending this card). He fights Jesse Arnett who is a really tough veteran and can really push him in every area. I am impressed with how alquaisi’s cardio and wrestling has looked in his last few, as he will need to rely on it to grind out arnett, who despite the record is actually pretty good, also only lost to top competition. Im not sure if the ufc would resign him, but he is one of the better 145ers not signed and is building up quite the solid resume. Idk why, but i could picture him going to PFL as well. The next fight is Abdulla Aliev. Aliev is 11-0 with 4 ko 2 sub. So he is interesting, because ive known about him for a while, but never saw him fight until he made his UAE warriors debut. He fought former ufc fighter Jenel Lausa. Now, Jenel is terrible and I expected him to steamroll him,and from what i remember, he was comfortably winning, but it wasnt all that exciting and it ended in a tech decision when he kicked lausa super hard in the nuts. He is an interesting case because he is a russian fighting in the chinese regional scene, but he has been one of the best guys out of there for some time, being the reigning WLF champ. I say this because his competition is actually quite good considering hes fighting in china. Hes beaten Road to ONE winner and now ONE fighter Zhaxi Zelang, has two wins over DWCS vet and road to ufc semifinalist Qiu Lun, and a win over ranked 125er sumudarjeri. Its also important to note these are his toughest guys and he finished qiulun twice and also finished su. Hes fought solid guys and his cardio has been tested, all thats needed is a quality performance. He will be tested against 12-3 Dias Erengepov, who is riding a 3 fight winstreak. Being a 125er and considering his resume, I think with a good performance, we could see aliev signed outright. Wouldnt shock me if he got dwcs but i think they would have enough reason to sign him. The next guy I have my eyes on is someone im very excited about in Murad Magomedov. Now, I am excited he got signed because he is a former ACA flyweight. Now, this is exciting because not only does ACA have one of the best 125 weight classes in the world, but ACA guys are incredibly hard to pick up, so he is nothing short of a hot commodity, especially for 125. He is 11-0 and has beaten guys like Zhifa Shang, felipe bunes, and Josiel Silva. He fights Alimarden Abdurakhamnov in a rematch from 2017 where murad won by 3rd round ko. It seems kind of pointless but, alimarden is coming off of a grand prix win where he went 3-0 with 3 kos against good competition, so he has clearly improved. I think Murad will also get signed outright and probably head directuly to the ufc (if they can get him that is). The final fight im looking at is the title fight between Vinicius Oliviera and Ali Taleb. Both guys are pretty good but I think Lok Dog is the real deal. I also think the winner of this is gonna be signed directly to the ufc, especially considering Lok Dog had a fight in front of dana white (didnt get signed lielly because of the short notice replacement). However, a win here, especially dominant, should do the trick. Lok Dog is 17-2 with 12 ko and 2 sub. He hasnt lost since 2018 and is on a 6 fight win streak where he has captured and defended the UAE Warriosr 135 strap. He was supposed to fight ray borg who is very good, so that shows u what UAE Warriors thinks of oliviera. He is all action and htis very hard so i think he would be a no brainer for the ufc to sign and also looks very legit. LFA 135 - Friday As pretty much every week, LFA has the most fighters i like and the best prospects and matchups. Despite being listed, the Lena Bishop vs Luana Santos fight is off because santos got signed to dwcs. First, we have An Ho. Ho is only 1-0, but he is coming off of a crazy spinning back fist in his pro and LFA debut. He had a 7-0 record that saw him claim the tuff-n-uff 125 ammy belt. He trains out of the MMA Lab, where their guys are alway crazy well rounded, and has shown that skillset throughout his pro and ammy career. Hes also only 21 so i am very intrigued with this kid. Hes just fighting a can, so this is just a fight for experience but hopefully he gets another highlight reel finish. Maria Henderson, the wife of Benson Henderson, is also fighting on this card, making her pro debut. Im not totally sure how good she is skillwise, but it has been fun to watch her fight throughout her amatuer career. She also trains at the mma lab and has shown a very solid grappling game thus far. Like her teammate, Ho, this is basically just an experience fight for her. Even if shes not that great, if she wins fights we will likely see her on dwcs. We have the return of LFA 135 title challenger, Allan Begoso. He is also getting basically a setup fight, but it will be good for his career. He hasnt fought since he lost to top prospect richard palencia, but hes only 26 so the time off is good. His wins had a combined 24-5 record, so he is fighting very good competition, has a great ground game, and trains at team alpha male. Hes gonna run through stanford (hopefully i dont curse him ahaha). Hopefully with a few more wins we see him get picked up by some org or get a dwcs shot. Next we have what very well could be a LFA title eliminator in Lucas Clay vs Austin Wourms. Clay is someone i rate as a pretty solid prospect and he is coming off of a razor close loss to another very good prospect in Aaron Mckenzie. Clay trains at St. Charles MMA, which is a gym im really fond of, and has fought very good competition, beating guys like mike breeden, JJ Okanovich, and Brant Moore. Despite the ugly record, he is fighting a solid opponent who has some good hype on him, subbing his last three opponents with first round armbars. He is 2-0 in LFA, beating two very good prospects as a massive underdog. However, i think the streak will end here as clay is a very competent grappler in his own right. One of my top 5 prospects is fighting on this card in Marcus McGhee. He is very interesting because he has been very inconsistent in his career, with long breaks inbetween fights. But as a pro, he has notched 4 fights in the past two years which is nice. Despite the inexperience and age (hes 34 which is very old for a prospect), he is very impressive to me. He trains at the mma lab which, if you couldnt tell, is a gym i rate highly. He is 4-0 as a pro with 4 kos and has ran through everyone hes fought. He is a pure striker but has good tdd, great balance, and has a pretty good get up game. He excels in the striking however; hes one of those guys you watch and you can just tell he knows what hes doing. Hes always moving and giving you different looks, but also throws incredible volume without gassing. He has beautiful combinations, has tons of power, and mixes shot selection very well. He fights his best test in 6-1n(should be 7-0 but had a dq loss) Rafael do Nascimento. I expect him to dominate and get on dwcs. He may be 34, but he has little damage on the body and is very athletic so i think he can fight for a solid amount of time, hes also hungry which dana likes. Now, the main event is a prospect ive been high on for the last year in Alfonso Leyva. He is 5-0 with 5 kos and trains out of entram gym. He competed at the 2016 and 2020 Olympics and has since switched over to MMA. Funny enough, he hasnt used his wrestling at all, instead striking and showing ridiculous power. I would like to see his wrestling in mma, but im sure its obviously very good. He hasnt fought great competition, but gets his stiffest test in 8-1 Daniel reis. I think being in the main event slot, he is a guy who could outright get signed to the ufc, as i think he is super talented, but dwcs would also make sense

posts.author: JReynolds47

posts.author: random knowledge
posts.body: Anybody know whats happening with Roberto Soldic? Last I heard he was in contract negotiations.

posts.author: Fremen
posts.body: His contract with KSW is ending, talked with UFC, been to Japan to see ONE championship, from what I know the KSW owners have made recently/will do soon the final talks with Soldic to decide his future as he currently holds 2 belts and they expect him to fight so he doesn't block both of these golds.

posts.author: Fremen
posts.body: My guess is that he will join the UFC later this year. 1. He is seeing what Gamrot has done after leaving KSW (he was a double champ as well)2. I believe UFC sees a big potential in him when it comes to marketing (dude already has big numbers, balkans is a new area to fight for, possibly a new legend after crocop)3. His level is above anyone in Europe so its hard to find him a decent opponent as long as he goes to the lightweight where weight comes to play. (He is WW and MW champion)4. Soldic is a very ambitious sportsman, he works to be the best in the world, and to be so you have to be in the UFC

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: @drchaotic it’s owned by ramazan khadyrov who to put it simply, is a peice of ****. There’s also an embargo on them because of human rights violations with Kahdyrov so that’s what makes it so hard to get ahold of them. Plus, most of the Russians there almost never leave the promotion so when they do, it’s a big deal. ACA guys are almost always high quality. You do occasionally get bums like duraev (although he would be better if he didn’t have 3 years off from contract issues imo) but then u have guys like yan, zabit, keremov, etc. they also have one of the best 125 divisions of any promotion imo. Third to UFC and ONE

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Hamdy adbelwahab just got signed to the ufc. 3-0 with 3 ko’s but also went 2-0 with 2 ko’s in masvidals bare knuckle mma (so basically, 5-0 with 5 ko). He is a former Olympic wrestler who placed 10th in the 2016 Olympics. He has can crushed so far, but has absolutely demolished everyone he’s fought and has looked very impressive thus far. He’s also very active, fighting 5 times as a pro in the last 8 months. Ufc is desperately in need of 265 talent and he is very atheltic with a wrestlign base, so he could develop into a problem. I will say, it’s weird he didn’t get signed to dwcs but I’m not complaining. I like him a lot but we need to see how he looks against ufc level comp. He can beat some of the shutters on the roster no question. I do seriously think he could develop into a contender tho; he has been very promising and has been seriously dominant in his career * Edited at 06.27.2022, 7:04 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Speaking of talent in the heavier weight classes, there was this guy I saw on the most recent Titan fc card, robelis despagnie, who really stood out to me. He was making his mma debut, so obviously he looked pretty green but the base for success looks there. Dude is cuban, so you know he’s got absurd genetics, standing at 6”7 with an 86 inch reach (yes you are reading that correctly) so just an absolute physicial specimen. He is a bit old for a prospect at 33, but it’s 265 so that’s not a worry and the reason for this late entry is he is a taekwando Olympian, winning bronze at the 2012 Olympics. He also bronze medaled at the 2013 and 15 world championships. I know taekwando is actually a terrible base for mma, but it means he’s freakishly atheltic, moves very well for a big guy, and can throw lots of unorthodox kicks. He could develop into something potentially Oh, and how could I forget the best part? posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: I was dumb enough to write about BFL and LFA but totally forgot they happen this week, not last, so here they are again + events that are actually happening this weekBFL 73 - Thursday The prospect i have my eyes on here is Navid Zanageh. So this one is actually fascinating to me because nobody knows about this kid and hes very under the radar. So why am i interested in a guy who 1. Nobody knows about and 2. Has 0 mma experience at any level? Well my friends, this is because Navid is a former member of Iran’s U23 wrestling team. Not only that, he has won gold at the Asian Games, a Silver Medal at the 2019 U23 Asian games, and a bronze at 2018 worlds. Hes only 25 years as well, so he hasnt even hit his athletic prime. Clearly, BFL thinks very highly of him because they are throwing him right into a 5 round title fight, against a guy with 13 fights combined pro and ammy (5-3 pro, 4-0 ammy). Now, obviously hes not fighting a good guy, but going from 0 experience in mma at any level, to fighting for a title against a guy with lots of experience is a pretty tough test for a debut. If he wins, he is definitely someone to look out for, especially fighting in a stacked division like 155LFA 135 - Friday As pretty much every week, LFA has the most fighters i like and the best prospects and matchups. Despite being listed, the Lena Bishop vs Luana Santos fight is off because santos got signed to dwcs. First, we have An Ho. Ho is only 1-0, but he is coming off of a crazy spinning back fist in his pro and LFA debut. He had a 7-0 record that saw him claim the tuff-n-uff 125 ammy belt. He trains out of the MMA Lab, where their guys are alway crazy well rounded, and has shown that skillset throughout his pro and ammy career. Hes also only 21 so i am very intrigued with this kid. Hes just fighting a can, so this is just a fight for experience but hopefully he gets another highlight reel finish. Maria Henderson, the wife of Benson Henderson, is also fighting on this card, making her pro debut. Im not totally sure how good she is skillwise, but it has been fun to watch her fight throughout her amatuer career. She also trains at the mma lab and has shown a very solid grappling game thus far. Like her teammate, Ho, this is basically just an experience fight for her. Even if shes not that great, if she wins fights we will likely see her on dwcs. We have the return of LFA 135 title challenger, Allan Begoso. He is also getting basically a setup fight, but it will be good for his career. He hasnt fought since he lost to top prospect richard palencia, but hes only 26 so the time off is good. His wins had a combined 24-5 record, so he is fighting very good competition, has a great ground game, and trains at team alpha male. Hes gonna run through stanford (hopefully i dont curse him ahaha). Hopefully with a few more wins we see him get picked up by some org or get a dwcs shot. Next we have what very well could be a LFA title eliminator in Lucas Clay vs Austin Wourms. Clay is someone i rate as a pretty solid prospect and he is coming off of a razor close loss to another very good prospect in Aaron Mckenzie. Clay trains at St. Charles MMA, which is a gym im really fond of, and has fought very good competition, beating guys like mike breeden, JJ Okanovich, and Brant Moore. Despite the ugly record, he is fighting a solid opponent who has some good hype on him, subbing his last three opponents with first round armbars. He is 2-0 in LFA, beating two very good prospects as a massive underdog. However, i think the streak will end here as clay is a very competent grappler in his own right. One of my top 5 prospects is fighting on this card in Marcus McGhee. He is very interesting because he has been very inconsistent in his career, with long breaks inbetween fights. But as a pro, he has notched 4 fights in the past two years which is nice. Despite the inexperience and age (hes 34 which is very old for a prospect), he is very impressive to me. He trains at the mma lab which, if you couldnt tell, is a gym i rate highly. He is 4-0 as a pro with 4 kos and has ran through everyone hes fought. He is a pure striker but has good tdd, great balance, and has a pretty good get up game. He excels in the striking however; hes one of those guys you watch and you can just tell he knows what hes doing. Hes always moving and giving you different looks, but also throws incredible volume without gassing. He has beautiful combinations, has tons of power, and mixes shot selection very well. He fights his best test in 6-1n(should be 7-0 but had a dq loss) Rafael do Nascimento. I expect him to dominate and get on dwcs. He may be 34, but he has little damage on the body and is very athletic so i think he can fight for a solid amount of time, hes also hungry which dana likes. Now, the main event is a prospect ive been high on for the last year in Alfonso Leyva. He is 5-0 with 5 kos and trains out of entram gym. He competed at the 2016 and 2020 Olympics and has since switched over to MMA. Funny enough, he hasnt used his wrestling at all, instead striking and showing ridiculous power. I would like to see his wrestling in mma, but im sure its obviously very good. He hasnt fought great competition, but gets his stiffest test in 8-1 Daniel reis. I think being in the main event slot, he is a guy who could outright get signed to the ufc, as i think he is super talented, but dwcs would also make sense Babilon MMA Lukasz Soldaski - 9-1 5 ko 1 sub. Lukasz fought on dwcs last year and unfortunately got ko’ed in the first round. He’s not a world beater but he has looked very solid in Babilon and is fighting for their 205 belt this week. Since losing, he rebounded with a split decision over polish vet, Michal Pasternak. His opponent this week isn’t notable, but he is coming off of a big upset win against formerly undefeated, dwcs and TUF alum Kenneth Bergh. Beatdown Promotions 1 Junior Tafa - 0-0. The brother of UFC heavyweight, Justin tafa makes his mma debut this week. Tafa was supposed to fight on dwcs last year, but that fell through (probably for the best tbh). Now, he’s making his mma debut on the regional scene. Tafa was a former glory kickboxer where he held a record of 23-5 with 19 ko’s. Unlike his brother, tafa is in much better physical shape, standing at 6”3 236 lbs. it will be interesting to see how he looks (he should look good considering he’s fighting a can). Considering they wanted to sign him with no experience, it will be interesting to see what the ufc does should he win Elliott Compton - 0-0. Here, we have another former kickboxer making their mma debut in Elliott Compton. CoMpton was a former ranked contender in ONE holding a MT/KB record of 46-14. He is a bit old for someone making their mma debut, at the ripe age of 33, but Compton has been doing bjj since the age of 10 and also owns his own mma gym, so he has some practice with the sport. He is getting a pretty tough debut fight in facing an 11-6 vet, likely because beatdown wants to push him considering he’s gotten into the game late. Should be interesting to see how he looks ACC 11 Assia Miri - 1-0. Assia trains at the MMA Factory in Paris and, like just about everybody at that gym, she has a striking background. Her sport is Muay Thai and she held a record of 103-19-3 and was a two time world champion, all at the very young age of 18 (she may be 19 now but I don’t remember; either way, she’s very young). She looked great in her mma debut, putting on a striking clinic for a 30-27. Now, she gets a big step up fighting a girl who is 4-0 (granted she’s can crushed but still, it’s a good record). Being so young and active in the sport, we could see her on the big stage soon Shamrock FC 339 Dustin Lampros - 7-0 5 ko 1 sub. Dustin competed on TUF 29 last year and lost by ko to vince Murdock (mega can and a loss that has aged like milk). He remains unbeaten as a pro and has aligned with sanford mma for his last few. He is fighting for the shamrock fc 145 belt, unfortunately fighting another not so great fighter (although it is a step up compared to what he’s normally faced). Being a TUF guy, we could probably see him get a dwcs opportunity sooner than later, but he’s really gotta beat someone with a pulse or at least fight in lfa to help get back to where he needs to be. MMA Grand Prix Delyan Gieorgiev - 1-0 1 ko. Gieorgiev is really promising imo. He is only 1-0 as a pro, but he holds a reported 17-1 ammy career (28-1 according to him) with his only loss being a split decision, so likely could be 18-0 or 29-0 (I believe 29-0 considering a lot of ammy stuff is not reported). Now, he wasn’t just any old amatuer, as he spent the majority of his time in the imaaf, picking up the 2017 European championship, 2017 world championship, 2018 world championship, and the 2019 world bronze medal. Besides immaf, he is also a Bulgarian adcc gold medalist, so he has grappling to go along with his wrestling. He also beat some really good amateurs like enrique hecher sosa, eduard kexel, and juan Carlos izuiqerdo. He fights 2-0 ibrahim idigov. Delyan may be someone to look out for if he keeps his winning ways Cedric doumbe- 1-0 1 ko. My favorite prospect fighting this week, Cedric doumbe, continues his foray into mma. The name may ring a bell from his unbelievable kickboxing career, where he was the two time Glory WW champ and was consistently ranked the #1 WW in the world up until he transitioned to mma. Cedric holds a record of 75-7-1 and racked up 5 title defenses as a champ, he also was the WAKO 170 champ, and won titles in K-1. He has many notable wins such as murthel groenhart (2x), nieky holtzken (2x), brad riddell, Jimmy Vienot, karim Ghajji, and thongchai sittsongpeenong. Doumbe has managed to accomplish all this, and hasn’t even reach his prime yet (29 years old at the moment). He is fighting a can, but it’s just someone to get him more experience, I’m sure next fight he will get better opposition. I expect to see Cedric get to the big leagues if he continues winning, he is very good and being so young, he doesn’t need to be rushed either so he could be a beast by the time we see him in a major org * Edited at 07.03.2022, 4:11 PM ET *

posts.body: Oh man manny was awesome, he really looked like a force at 135/145 but he couldn’t keep his weight in check. He’s been on the sidelines for a while and hasn’t posted training pics at all on his ig so not sure where his head is at. If he fights again, he’s definitely gonna be fighting on CES/NEF there’s also some promotion that just started up that was founded by a big player in NE MMA (I forget the name lol) so he could get picked up there as well. I’m not sure if the ufc will want him again given the weight issues but he’s super talented so we could see him back there if he keeps fighting and winning. Wouldn’t be surprised if he got picked up by pfl/Bellator as well. Really hope he fights again, he was an awesome prospect

posts.body: All the bantamweights are fighting each other (even some of the prospects like Shore and Simon) which is good, can't say the same for some other divisions COUGH COUGH WELTERWEIGHT COUGH but man this is kinda dog**** matchmaking IMO. Who asked for Vera vs Cruz and Aldo vs Merab? Like they're good fight between contenders, but they're probably the most unappealing matches you could've made. I would've done Cruz vs Aldo, battle of the legends. A dream match between the former 135 and 145 champs. O Malley vs Vera rematch, winnable fight for Sean that's still a big step up and a desired matchup. Merab vs Yan. I mean come on it just makes sense. Test out Yan's grappling to an even higher extent by putting him against Aljo's wrestler friend, plus can serve as a title eliminator. That's just my opinion but I think the UFC really dropped the ball on this one

posts.author: JoseLobo
posts.body: The league boasts a long and varied list of investors, including Ted Leonsis, the owner of Washington’s N.B.A., W.N.B.A. and N.H.L. teams; Glenn Youngkin, the governor of Virginia; the rapper Wiz Khalifa; and the comedian Kevin Hart.In May, the P.F.L. secured $30 million in funding from a group that included the Alex Rodriguez, the former baseball player who is also part of Minnesota’s N.B.A. and W.N.B.A. ownership groups. The league also has a broadcast deal with ESPN.Harrison signed In March with the Professional Fighters League, competing in the 155-pound lightweight division. The two-year contract made her the highest-paid fighter both in the P.F.L. promotion and in women’s M.M.A. Harrison will gross roughly $1 million per fight, and can earn another $1 million if she wins this year’s P.F.L. championship.Kayla is the face of the PFL, like it or not. What is certain is that investors understand business and she is likely to make about 4 million a year. It's funny how an MMA company that hires experienced fighters and offers good money gets criticized.

posts.body: The french commision states that fighters with under 10 fights are not to be seperated by 4 or more fights as they believe it creates a mismatch or is used to pad records. posts.author: DrChaotic
posts.body: I severely doubt that Adesanya will be champion by the time Bo reaches the top, and even if he is he's like the easiest stylistic matchup possible. I don't think being in a division with weak wrestlers matters for Bo though. He's not Pereira, and he's not some mid guy who relies solely on his above average wrestling. He could be in the most grappling heavy division and I think he'd out grapple 99% of the guys there. Whittaker is the only real nightmare matchup for him, but Whittaker's a nightmare matchup for anyone who's not an amazing striker with good takedown defense

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: I think they might take their time with him tbh. Not that he necessarily needs it but I don’t think we’re gonna see him go down the pereira path of 3 wins and a title shot. Especially given the fact he’s only 26. 185 is really shallow so naturally he gets like 4/5 wins and he’s already ranked but I don’t think he’s gonna get rushed to the top

posts.author: DrChaotic
posts.body: Also I don't see why the UFC would fast track him to the title shot. He's a young up and coming prospect. Sure he's not 22, but he's way closer to that than a 35 year old Pereira. Plus there's no history with Adesanya. I'm not saying they're gonna make him go 10-0 before getting a ranked contender, but I really doubt they'll fast track him to the title shot either. Maybe 2 or 3 solid wins, ranked person, top 10 guy, and then a top 5 contender before getting the title shot at around 8-0

posts.author: DrChaotic
posts.body: Evloev vs Mitchell is a contender dream match and it's gonna happen. It better happen. Nobody in the UFC cares about their "perfect records". That's the kind of **** that made me hate undefeated people in the first place, never wanting to fight guys who could potentially take their 0 unless it's a title fight. Thankfully this isn't very common outside of boxing (minus Russians), but yeah I think it gets booked (hopefully). Who else are they gonna fight? I see people going "nOOOooo this is a bad matchup don't kill off one prospect feed Zombie to Mitchel battle of the twisters and do Evloev vs someone coming off a loss like Kattar" and to those people I say "kindly ****". They're both high enough in the rankings where they don't have anymore "easy" options (think Zombie, maybe someone coming off a loss like Ortega. Kattar is a nightmare matchup and is always motivated so his loss doesn't matter, he wouldn't be an "easy" fight regardless). They're both past the point of being protected prospects. This isn't Khamzat vs Shavkat when they were both 1-0 in the UFC and unranked. These dudes are both ranked in the top 10, and 1 more win after this practically guarantees a title shot. Book that **** immediately Dana

posts.author: DrChaotic
posts.body: Yooooooo! Daniel Marcos! Where tf did that signing come from lmao. I mean I’m not gonna complain, I’ve been wanting to see him lose his 0 for a while since I really dislike people who sit out for 2 years and only post on Facebook “UFC I’m ready give me an opportunity”. Idk about his opponent though, he’s got half the wins and a loss, but he’s still barely an underdog on tapology so far (given it’s been less than a day and the gap will probably increase as time goes by)I’ve been looking for an excuse to ask about this for a while, and now this is my time. What ever happened to Sergio Giglio? Another 12-0 Peruvian who’s at the same gym/friends with Marcos. Hasn’t fought in even LONGER than Marcos (since like 2018) but he’s so ******* young (25) and was actually signed to the UFC at one point. Had a whole fight scheduled and everything but pulled out with an injury, and then just randomly got released one day years later. Maybe. I never saw anything about him getting released, but one day around June 2021 he just wasn’t on the roster anymore (I went through old “current UFC fighters” Wikipedia logs)What happened to the dude? Just disappeared out of nowhere for seemingly no reason, just like Guam Wang. I wonder where they both are (though I heard somewhere that Guam retired and runs a business in China)

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: So, we always talk about promising pro prospects, but let me tell you about an amateur who is allegedly being scouted by ONE Championship (so ive been told). His name is Delton Kaufman, he is 15 years old and trains at power mma with Ryan Bader and the crew (also been to the mma lab) and holds an amateur record of 26-1, all under USFL (U.S. offshoot of imaaf) and IMMAF. He is the two time immaf world champ (just won 3 days ago), 3 time USFL champ, won multiple state wrestling titles, and also a 4 time ibjjf pan am gold medalist. all this at the age of 15. Obviously, because hes a child, his record isn't listed on topology but this kid is an under the radar beast and will definitely be a serious problem in the future. Remember this post * Edited at 08.22.2022, 7:48 PM ET *

posts.author: DrChaotic
posts.body: It's kinda weird to think about great fighters or fighters on huge win streaks that will most likely lose their next fight. The ONE card is going on right now and I'm thinking to myself "Huh. RDR is probably gonna lose his next fight". I don't think he has much of a chance against Malykhin. And if Bhullar somehow wins that I doubt he has much chance against him either. But more importantly... The PFP number 1 and current longest win streak in the UFC since Usman lost? (idk fact check me on that) Alexander Volkanovski. It's more likely than not that he losses his next fight and that win streak will come to an end (and holy **** maybe Oliveira will be pfp #1... please get through Islam). As a Do Bronx fan it pains me to say this but he has a better chance against Charles than Islam, but I don't think he wins either of those, ESPECIALLY if/when Charles beats Islam. If Islam wins (god let's pray that this doesn't happen) then Volk has an even smaller chance, there I think it's like 90% guaranteed he loses. He can do absolutely nothing to stop the bigger man from absolutely mauling him on the ground. * Edited at 08.26.2022, 10:24 AM ET *

posts.author: DrChaotic
posts.body: It's just kinda cool and weird to think about. Like "oh yeah I didn't even realize the biggest guy in the sport skill wise is probably gonna lose his next fight and relinquish that title and his UFC win streak... huh. neat"

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Some exciting news, not really mma related but exciting nonetheless. So, many of you asked me to start a YouTube channel, I tried coming up with video ideas and working on editing and whatnot, but found it kind of difficult. Instead, I have now launched an instagram page where I will be going fully in-depth on prospects that I like each week. Here is an excerpt from my first post https://www.instagram.com/mma.prospec****ch/Name: Jose Perez Weight: 145 Record: 7-1 Finishes: 4 submission Accolades: CFFC Featherweight Champion Team: @gilbertgrappling Bio: Jose Perez is allergic to boring fights. The 27 year old fighting out of Gilbert Grappling in Illinois has been working his way up the ranks in the regional scene and is knocking on the door of a major promotion. The newly crowned CFFC featherweight champion has been on a tear recently, building up a three fight winstreak that included a win over fellow prospect, Paul Capauldo. Jose Perez has a very fan friendly style, not fearing away from a striking battle on the feet, or a grappling match on the ground. One thing that really stands out to me in Perez is how much he has improved his striking. In his last two fights, he has mostly stayed on the feet and really impressed me against Paul Capauldo. This was a big fight for Perez because Capauldo had a lot of hype on him at the time; Paul was coming off of a highlight reel wheel kick KO, and was going to be on DWCS, but a case of Covid prevented that. Perez played spoiler in a back-and-forth war that culminated into a dominant win and one of the bloodiest fights you will see in the sport. Most expected the grappler to wrestle and avoid the fast hands of Capauldo; instead, Perez walked him down and showed that he can strike too. When the fight got to the mat, Perez showed very impressive grappling, as to be expected. I am very impressed when i watch Perez’s grappling because he can flow effortlessly on the ground. He is very good at getting into mount and is very creative in his submission game. He is always looking for the finish and is dangerous everywhere, even picking up a win by the rare banana split. After the Capauldo fight, Perez went on to win the CFFC Featherweight Championship and put his name out there as one of the best prospects out there. He is not only a must watch fighter with a fan friendly style, he is also an extremely talented fighter and would fit right in with the UFC’s stacked Featherweight division.

posts.author: jrscolwell
posts.body: Something interesting I just found: After tonight's card I was thinking about how many Brazilian fighters we've seen on DWC this season, so I went back to count. We always expect USA-based fighters to be the most frequent just because of where the show and the UFC itself is located, and by my count there were 30 (or 31 if you count Karl Williams but I consider the Virgin Islands their own thing) that have either fought or are scheduled to fight this year. But what's crazy is how close Brazil is this year, I counted 29 Brazilians! And with 10 fighters per week, 10 weeks, there's only 100 slots total, meaning that the USA+Brazil have more representation than the rest of the world combined and almost 1/3rd of the total fighters are from each country.

posts.author: jrscolwell
posts.body: Not exactly shocking considering those are 2 of the big 3 talent-producing countries, but I don't ever remember a year being so dominated by two countries. And its also interesting looking at the numbers for the third big country, Russia. Now obviously there's major complications with a war going on, but I still find it incredible that with all of the talent only 2 Russians made it onto the show this year: Viktoriya Dudakova and Ikram Aliskerov. I've long suspected that the UFC has a limit of how many Russians, especially wrestlers, they're willing to sign, because if they were really just taking the best prospects in the world there could easily be 15-20 different guys each year from Dagestan or Chechnya with big beards and Magomed somewhere in their names.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: ONE had a presser in Thailand today. Lots of exciting news Starting in 2023, ONE is going to have 52 shows (1 every week) at Lumpinee stadium (2nd biggest Muay Thai stadium in Thailand) every Friday. No updates on if it’s strictly Muay Thai or if it’s mma too but either way it’s good. They also pledged 100 million investment into the sport of Muay Thai (whatever that means and how they got 100 mil is beyond me). They also announced ONE: warrior series is back. This time in the Philippines where 8 guys will attempt to win a contract. They also announced more road to ONE competitions, targeting Canada, United Kingdom, Germany, Mongolia, Russia, South Africa, Australia, Philippines, Japan, and U.S.A Finally, ONE announced another mixed rules (mma/Muay Thai) fight. This time stamp Fairtex vs Anissa Meksen and it takes place on their 6th prime show in Thailand, Jan 14 * Edited at 09.12.2022, 1:13 PM ET *

posts.author: Tieoken
posts.body: @Fish, I know it’s a lot later but I just now saw this.Loop choke? In no-GI MMA. I think the baseball bat choke is a modified form of it, but you don’t see it because without the GI you are able to rotate your way out of it more easily. But the main reason imo is that with the gloves on it makes interlocking your fingers to get the wrist leverage to complete the blood choke impossible to hold on unless you got some green bean fingers.

posts.author: CheckLeftHook
posts.body: yeah O'Malley popped for the same thing before UFC 229 but he said it was a "tainted supplement", but he had like a broken ankle or something from the Andre fight I think, so he just tried to rush the recovery to make it on the McGregor card but got popped like a month before or something. Anyways my point is, they're taking a PED but it's one of those that's used for quick recovery from an injury, I don't think there is any sort of actual "enhancement" being done to their performance, they just get to come back from rehab faster.

posts.author: Theeraser
posts.body: yea i had to explain to my friends who dont really watch that popping is more common then not guys can pop for taking a ******* advil

posts.author: Theeraser
posts.body: before this year you were getting suspended for ******* weed dumbest **** ever

posts.author: CheckLeftHook
posts.body: so this ostarine thing made me look at O'Malley a bit closer and it seems he really did take the drug to try and recover an injury and then also possibly build some strength too (dude gets hurt like every fight, so yeah)in 2018 he pops before UFC 229 and is removed from the card. he goes on to say that he apparently bought some caffeine pills and they were contaminated with this...you know, very specific drug that would be very beneficial to him and his injury at the time. he serves his USADA suspension, but then a year later for UFC 239 he pops again for "trace amounts" of ostarine. This time though USADA will not impose anymore sanctions, and they go on to say that it's "likely" the trace amounts are from the year prior. Like they really just did the absolute bare minimum required of them the second time around. But then you go online and you can find **** like this: https://paradigmpeptides.com/product/ostarine-mk-2866/Blatantly selling the drug in pill form for $50 lolbut the funniest part to me is this: "Terms: The products we offer are intended for laboratory research use only." I love how blatant it all is and out in the open. Anyways safe to say they are cheating intentionally. The further you dig into their stories the less and less their excuses make sense. Like if we're to look at it from USADA's point of view, they have now found THREE separate PED's that "pulse" - ostarine, clomiphene, and turinabol, yet out of all the people caught only barely a handful of people are all of the sudden "pulsing" lol give me a break, **** the Golden Snitch

posts.author: CheckLeftHook
posts.body: also if you're an athlete getting drug tested please stay away from: - Gas station **** pills: you could get popped for clomiphene or turinabol (ask Jon Jones) or drostanolone and androsterone (ask Anderson Silva) - Caffeine pills: you can get popped for ostarine (ask Sean O'Malley) now bare with me here- organic, vegan, plant-based, daily multivitamin: you can get popped for ligandrol (ask Nate Diaz)safe to say I'm bored today

Nice to have a really good backup plan though
